title: Slot-pooling for MSA redline handoff and onboarding checklist
preview:
From: Jordan Miles <procurement@aventislogistics.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Garcia <maria.garcia@aventislogistics.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 12:18:00 -0400
Subject: MSA redline handoff — proposed slots

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the intro earlier. Aventis is ready to walk through the MSA redline and align on onboarding next steps. We anticipate 60 minutes for the session to cover redline walkthrough + action items.

Proposed windows (ET):
- Tue May 18 — 11:00–12:00
- Wed May 19 — 09:00–10:00
- Thu May 20 — 15:00–16:00

If none of those work, happy to take a couple of alternative times from your side. Please include anyone from legal and our procurement owner (Maria) on the invite.

I attached a brief summary of our outstanding changes (prelim_redline.pdf) to help guide the call.

Best,
Jordan Miles
Procurement, Aventis Logistics
attachments: prelim_redline.pdf


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Miles <procurement@aventislogistics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:02:00 -0400
Subject: Re: MSA redline handoff — proposed slots

Hi Jordan — thanks for sending this over and for the summary.

We usually run a 45–60 minute redline handoff but to avoid a single point-of-failure on scheduling I wanted to propose a \"slot-pool\" approach: provide 3 adjacent half-hour windows over two days and we pick the one that best suits cross-team availability. That tends to reduce back-and-forth when legal or engineering needs to swap.

Our available slot-pool (ET):
- Mon May 17 — 10:30–11:15 or 11:30–12:15 or 13:00–13:45
- Tue May 18 — 09:00–09:45 or 10:00–10:45 or 11:00–11:45

Required attendees: Aventis legal + procurement, Redwood legal (Laura) and CS (Ben) for onboarding checkpoints.

I've attached our working draft of the redline (MSA_aventis_redline_v1.docx) and an internal onboarding checklist so we can surface any pre-call asks. If you confirm a preferred pool/time, we will send a calendar invite and agenda.

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
phone: +1 415-555-0198
attachments: MSA_aventis_redline_v1.docx, vendor_onboarding_checklist.pdf

title: Renewal & capacity uplift — paperwork and MSA redline coordination
preview:
From: Sam Torres <sam.torres@deeplytics.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Renewal + 60% capacity increase — next steps for paperwork

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We ran the numbers and want to proceed with the renewal + a 60% capacity increase across our inference pods starting July 1. Attached is the order form Deeplytics filled out with the requested uplift and proposed billing cadence.

We need to align on term length (we're leaning 24 months) and a couple of legal items before procurement can issue PO:
- Data retention / deletion language for cached KV data
- SLA credits application for burst capacity
- A short confidentiality addendum for model weights exchange during onboarding

Can you loop in legal and let us know an estimated timeline to get a redlined MSA back? Our procurement lead is procurement@deeplytics.com.

Attachments: Deeplytics_OrderForm_2027-05-03.pdf (pdf)

Best,
Sam Torres
Head of ML Ops, Deeplytics


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <sam.torres@deeplytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + 60% capacity increase — next steps for paperwork

Sam — thanks for the clear summary and the order form. We're excited to support the uplift. Quick outline of next steps from our side:

1) Redwood will generate an updated Order Form + redline to our standard MSA and return to you by EOD 2027-05-06.
2) Ben (legal) — please review the two customer asks (data retention / SLA credits) and flag any outbound negotiation positions.
3) Monica (CS/ops) — I'll work with you to confirm the exact instance sizing behind the 60% uplift (seats, GPU types, KV storage) so the order form matches provisioning.
4) Laura (revops) — can you prepare billing cadence options (monthly vs quarterly vs annual prepay) and call out any promo/credit we can offer for 24-month term?

From Redwood's perspective, a 24-month term is fine — we usually ask to keep indemnity and liability caps unchanged. For data retention, we can offer a 90-day KV cache wipe by default, with an option to contractually shorten to 30 days if necessary (would like legal to confirm language). For SLA credits on burst traffic, we prefer preserving the existing credit metric (monthly window) but can add an explicit carve for burst overage triggers.

Proposed call: 2027-05-06 10:00 PT (30m) to run through the redlines and procurement path. Does that work for your team? If procurement prefers email-only redlines, we'll accept a tracked-change doc back.

Attachments: Redwood_MSA_v3_draft_redline.docx (redline)

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-05T08:40:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Deeplytics paperwork — quick legal points

Aisha / Ben / Laura — quick internal note after skimming Sam's order form:

- Data retention: counter-propose the 90-day default but add an optional Exhibit X for 30-day retention if customer requires. That keeps the template predictable and allows a one-off carve.
- SLA credits: keep monthly uptime window, but add language that credits do not apply to burst provisioning events triggered by customer-originated spikes (so we can rely on autoscale billing).
- Confidentiality addendum: small tweak to Section 5.2 to allow short-term access to model artifacts for onboarding, conditioned on a narrower purpose clause and audit logs.

Ben — can you prepare 2-3 terse alternatives for each point we can provide to Deeplytics next call? Also include an estimate if any of these materially change liability exposure (we should flag to Aisha if so).

Quoted from Sam's email for context:
> We need to align on term length (we're leaning 24 months) and a couple of legal items before procurement can issue PO:
> - Data retention / deletion language for cached KV data
> - SLA credits application for burst capacity
> - A short confidentiality addendum for model weights exchange during onboarding

Monica
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Varga <elena.varga@deeplytics.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Torres <sam.torres@deeplytics.com>, procurement@deeplytics.com
Date: 2027-05-10T15:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + 60% capacity increase — next steps for paperwork

Aisha — apologies for the delay, procurement got pulled into an audit last week. We reviewed Redwood's draft and have attached our redline to the MSA (Deeplytics_MSA_redline_v2.docx). High level:

- We accepted the 24-month term.
- Data retention: proposed stronger deletion guarantees (30 days) — we included language in the redline.
- SLA credits: we're willing to accept the monthly window but want explicit language that credits also apply if latency SLA misses occur during provisioning windows.
- Confidentiality addendum: attached a short addendum; procurement insists on a narrow scope and additional logging requirements.

Procurement asked that legal and CS route follow-ups via email and that we agree the final doc by 2027-05-14 to allow PO issuance and July 1 provisioning.

Attachments: Deeplytics_MSA_redline_v2.docx (docx, tracked changes)

Forwarded message:
From: Sam Torres <sam.torres@deeplytics.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T16:25:00-07:00
Subject: Deeplytics_OrderForm_2027-05-03.pdf
To: procurement@deeplytics.com

Please see the order form for the uplift.

Thanks,
Elena Varga
Director of Procurement, Deeplytics

title: SIG / CAIQ kickoff — scheduling windows and evidence roadmap
preview:
From: Naomi Blake <naomi.blake@ironshore.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: procure@ironshore.com
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG/CAIQ kickoff request + preliminary questionnaire

Hi Alex —

Thanks for the quick reply over LinkedIn. We're starting an audit track for a prospective customer (one of our enterprise partners) and they requested a SIG/CAIQ + SOC 2 readiness review of vendors. I've attached the initial questionnaire (Ironshore_SIG_Initial.pdf) — most of it is straightforward but the partner will want to review logging/retention and encryption specifics.

Can we schedule a 45-minute kickoff this week to:
- Walk through the CAIQ highlights and any non-standard controls
- Confirm artifact owners and log sample windows
- Get a timeline for Q&A and evidence delivery (we need artifacts by May 14)

Proposed windows in our timezone (PDT):
- Wed May 5, 10:00–10:45
- Wed May 5, 15:00–15:45
- Thu May 6, 11:00–11:45

Attendees from our side: Naomi Blake (Security PM), Samir Patel (Engineering), and procurement.

Please CC whoever on your side owns evidence (logs, key management, SOC artifacts). We also need an initial list of sample log ranges so we can request account-level access if needed.

Thanks,
Naomi Blake
Security Program Manager
Ironshore Analytics

Attachment: Ironshore_SIG_Initial.pdf (12 pages)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Naomi Blake <naomi.blake@ironshore.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ kickoff request + preliminary questionnaire

Thanks Naomi — welcome, and happy to help.

We can do Wed May 5 at 15:00 PDT (45 min). I've penciled Mina in — she leads our assurance intake — and Ben from customer success will own the logistics for artifact transfer.

Agenda (proposed):
1) Quick intros (5 min)
2) CAIQ/SIG areas that typically need clarification (10 min)
3) Evidence & log sampling windows + owners (15 min)
4) Timeline for deliverables and checklist (10 min)
5) Next steps / action owners (5 min)

Pre-work: Could you upload the questionnaire to a shared Drive link or confirm that the attached PDF is the full set? Also confirm which tenant/account(s) you want log samples for — if you can provide account ids we can map log windows quickly.

I've attached our intake template (redwood-evidence-matrix-template.xlsx) and a short instructions doc for log samples (log-sample-instructions.txt). We'll use those during the call to capture owners/locations.

Meeting link (Zoom): https://zoom.us/j/000-111-222 (will send calendar invite)

See you Wed 15:00 PDT.

— Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-evidence-matrix-template.xlsx, log-sample-instructions.txt


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
To: Naomi Blake <naomi.blake@ironshore.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 5 May 2027 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: SIG/CAIQ kickoff request + preliminary questionnaire

Hi Naomi / Alex —

Thanks for including me. Quick notes ahead of today's 15:00 PT call so we can be efficient:

1) Controls likely to surface: key management (KMS/HSM), multi-tenant isolation (dedicated vs hosted), logging retention and redaction procedures, and secret rotation cadence.

2) Evidence we commonly provide immediately:
- SOC 2 summary / snapshot (if available)
- Encryption-at-rest architecture diagram + KMS provider (we will provide a sanitized diagram)
- Sample logs: API access logs and inference request logs for three representative accounts (we need account ids; list least-privilege windows)
- Access control lists for console access (named owners)

3) Log sample guidance: please provide 3 non-overlapping 48-hour windows across your test and prod-equivalent tenants. We will scrub any PII and provide a redaction log. If you need logs with request/response payloads included, we will require an MSA addendum — let us know.

title: Request: region-specific retention + hashed-audit-export for Maple Financial
preview:
From: Anna Lopez <anna@maplefin.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 09:13:00 -0700
Subject: Audit logs / retention controls question

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — the team liked the latency profile and the model options. Before we route this to procurement we need a clearer picture on two items that are gating our SOC/Procurement review:

1) Per-region retention controls: We need the ability to set retention policies scoped to a customer and region (e.g., EU logs retained 90 days, US logs 365 days) and to ensure logs for EU workloads never leave EU storage.

2) Audit export with PII hashing: Our auditor asks that any exported audit trail be PII-masked (SHA-256 or equivalent) and include a verifiable export record (who exported, when, what filters were applied).

Can Redwood support a scoped export with hashed PII and region scoping, or do you have a recommended workaround? This is a showstopper for our security team.

If possible, please include a rough timeline — we're aiming to close in May if technical gating is solved. I've attached the SOW draft and our security questionnaire.

Thanks,
Anna Lopez
Head of AI Ops
Maple Financial

Attachments: MAPLE_SOW_v2.pdf (application/pdf), maple_security_questionnaire.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial (deal: DEAL-2027-0429-MAPLE)

Team — forwarding Maple's ask (see below). Quick summary: high ACV opportunity (~$1.1M ARR first year), procurement needs per-region retention + export with PII hashing and an audit trail. They want to close in May.

Can Product/Eng confirm feasibility and a minimal-scope implementation we could offer as a formal commitment? Options I see:
- Short path (2 weeks): export shim that hashes identifiable fields on export and stores export metadata in our existing audit log table; no regional enforcement (risk).
- Compliance path (6-10 weeks): retention policy per-tenant/region + region-aware export that pins storage zone and records exported columns + operator identity.

Asks:
1) Kimberly / Product: can we scope a lightweight export feature that meets Maple's auditor (hashed PII + export record) as an off-ramp while we work on true regional retention?
2) Rishi / Eng: rough estimate and dependencies (ops/storage, infra changes, Vault/KMS usage for hashing salt?).
3) Marissa / AE: I'll hold a stub commitment but don't want to promise regional isolation until we have a plan.

Proposed next step: 30-min triage tomorrow to align on what we can sign for a May close.

- Aditya

On Thu, Apr 22, 2027 at 09:13 AM Anna Lopez <anna@maplefin.com> wrote:
> [original message omitted]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 08:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial

Thanks for flagging — quick thoughts from Product:

- Compliance must-haves: (a) export provenance (who/when/filters), (b) deterministic PII hashing on export, (c) documented retention TTLs per-tenant.
- We should avoid promising region pinning in the same sprint unless Eng confirms storage zoning can be enforced without heavy infra changes.

Proposal (two-track):
A) Short-deliverable (3 weeks): \"Hashed Export + Export Audit Record\" feature behind a feature flag. Exports run through a server-side transform that hashes configured fields (configurable per-tenant), writes an export manifest to audit events, and produces a zip with manifest. This mitigates the auditor ask for PII. Does not enforce storage zoning — export artifacts will live in a controlled S3 bucket.

B) Mid-term (8-12 weeks): \"Region-aware retention and export\" that integrates with per-tenant storage routing and KMS-backed regional keys. That is a larger infra project and should be scheduled against roadmap items.

If Sales wants A as a contractual commitment, we should price it as an accelerated engineering change with a scope SLT (support window + limited beta).

Kimberly
Product Manager, Compliance

-----Original Message-----
(Forwarded content omitted)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 10:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial

TL;DR: Feasible but with caveats and infra touchpoints.

Engineering assessment:
- Hashed Export: LOW effort (approx 6 engineer-days) if we implement it as a transformation pipeline step before packaging the export. We will need: a config mapping per-tenant for fields to hash; a deterministic hash function (we propose HMAC-SHA256 with tenant-specific salt stored in KMS); export manifest writing to our audit-events topic; and an admin UI to trigger/monitor exports.
- Region-aware retention + storage pinning: MED-HIGH effort (6-10 engineer-weeks). Requires changes to our storage routing layer, creation of regional buckets, and adding per-tenant routing policies. Also compliance validation and e2e test automation.

Dependencies/notes:
1) KMS work: we must provision tenant-scoped salts/keys — coordination with Security and Infra.
2) Ops: export artifacts live in S3; for short-term we can use our existing encrypted bucket (US) and mark artifacts with a manifest indicating region-of-origin. Not ideal for EU retention guarantees.
3) Backport risk: if we ship hashed export quickly, we must ensure future regional migration doesn't re-expose raw data.

Estimate for the short path: engineering lead + one SWE to deliver in 2 calendar weeks (buffered estimate: 10 business days) including QA and basic admin UI.

I'll block time with Infra for a quick sync.

Rishi
Engineering Lead, Serving Runtime

> On Fri, Apr 23, 2027 at 08:27 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> > [proposal omitted]


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 16:31:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial

Thanks all — this is helpful. I spoke to Anna and Procurement this afternoon. They will accept the short path (hashed export + export manifest) as an interim contractual commitment if we can deliver within 3 weeks and provide a migration plan to region-aware retention within the next quarter.

Sales asks/constraints:
- Commitment visibility: We need a one-paragraph description of the short-path feature we can include in the SOW and an estimated delivery date (3 weeks from green light).
- Migration plan: a one-page bullet list describing how artifacts will be migrated/segregated once region-aware storage is available, and a rollback/fallback plan if we discover raw data surfaced in the interim.
- Beta access: Maple wants to be in a limited beta and will sign an accelerated change SOW if we attach a 90-day limited liability carveout and agreed operational window for support.

Can Product/Eng provide the one-paragraph SOW language and a tentative delivery date? If we get green light, I'll ask Legal to draft the short SOW amendment and Procurement will circulate.

-Aditya

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 11:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Audit logs / retention controls question — Maple Financial

Great — thanks team. Legal is on standby; Procurement confirmed they can sign an accelerated SOW once Product provides the short paragraph and Eng signs up for the 3-week window.

Draft SOW blurb (for Product to confirm):
\"Redwood will deliver a tenant-configurable export capability that performs deterministic hashing of configured PII fields (HMAC-SHA256 with tenant-scoped salt) and produces an export manifest capturing exporter identity, timestamp, and applied filters. This export feature will be offered in a limited beta to Maple Financial within 3 weeks of contract signature. Export artifacts will initially be stored in Redwood's encrypted US storage and will be migrated to region-specific storage as part of the Region-Aware Retention project (to be scheduled within the next quarter).\"

Action items:
- Kimberly/Rishi: confirm the blurb or propose edits by EOD.
- Monica: prepare ops runbook stub for export monitoring and retention tags.
- Ben/Aditya: prepare SOW amendment request for Legal.

I'll coordinate the Legal thread once the blurb is confirmed.

-Marissa

> > [Sales confirmation omitted]

title: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment and temporary rate shelter
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@eastlake.io>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal + expansion discussion — Eastlake pricing alignment

Hi Eleanor —

Thanks again for the call last week. As we head toward Eastlake's July 1 renewal, I wanted to get a formal note on the table with two objectives: lock a renewal rate for the upcoming quarter while also supporting your anticipated volume increase from the new recommendation engine pilot.

High level proposal (attached SOW + pricing workbook):

title: Post-demo: TTFT & streaming targets (recap + next steps)
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <psingh@stellarpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: TTFT & streaming targets (recap + next steps)

Priya — thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our call and proposed next steps so we can align on the pilot scope. Please confirm any corrections.

High-level ask from Stellar: ensure streaming responses meet production TTFT and mobile UX targets for your payments flows. From the call I captured the following target envelope you want to validate in a POC: 
- TTFT (time-to-first-token): <= 150 ms median, <= 250 ms p99 (mobile, US region)
- Baseline throughput: 50 request/s (avg), burst to 200 r/s when queued transactions spike
- Concurrency: support ~1k concurrent chat sessions initially; scale to 3k over 90 days
- Chunking / cadence: initial payload (first chunk) within 120 ms of request; subsequent chunks every ~125–200 ms to keep UI streaming smooth
- Preferred transport: SSE for server-driven flows (simpler retry model), but can accept WebSocket for mobile sessions if you require bi‑directional low-latency edits

Technical clarifications we need from you to size the pilot and runtime config: 
1) Typical prompt + response token sizes (avg and 95th pct) for the payments flow.
2) Expected payload size (MB) and whether you include attachments (receipts) in the streamed response.
3) Spike profile: how long do bursts last (seconds/minutes) and expected frequency per day/week.

Proposed next steps: 
- Ben: build a lightweight harness that simulates your mobile client with both SSE and WS options, capture TTFT & chunk cadence. Target delivery: 4 business days.
- Laura: run a 2-day sandbox pilot with a 50 r/s steady-state and two burst windows (5 min, 15 min). Schedule window options in calendar.
- Priya / Prod Team: send sample payloads and a small dataset (anonymized) we can use for token-sizing and latency profiling.

Attachments: SLO_proposal_Stellar_v1.pdf (proposed SLO table + fallback policy), demo-recording-20270422.mp4 (full demo).

If the targets above look off from your expectations, call them out and we’ll revise. Happy to jump on a quick 15-minute follow-up if that helps.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Singh <psingh@stellarpay.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Prod Team <prod@stellarpay.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 14:12:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: TTFT & streaming targets (recap + next steps)

Thanks Alex — good summary. A couple of clarifications from our side: 
- The 250 ms p99 is our hard goal for the checkout/authorization path; for non-critical notifications we can accept p99 up to 400 ms. So your proposed envelope is fine for checkout but please mark the critical path explicitly.
- Typical token sizes (avg): prompt ~40 tokens, response ~180 tokens; 95th pct response ~420 tokens.
- Payload size: most responses are <30 KB; receipts (rare) can be ~120 KB. We do not stream binary attachments in the SSE stream — we hand off a URL after the final token.
- Spike profile: bursts usually last 2–6 minutes during regional promotions; we see 3–4 such bursts per week.

On transport: mobile engineering prefers WebSocket because we already have a persistent socket and want to avoid negotiating SSE on intermittent networks. However, we are open to SSE for web UI. For the POC, could you run both transports and show comparative TTFT/packetization numbers?

We also need to thread security and compliance: please include your SOC2 reference and a short note on how cached prompts are isolated per-tenant. Our security lead will request a questionnaire soon.

I can share a small anonymized set of 200 sample prompts/responses by EOD. Looking forward to the pilot dates.

— Priya
Priya Singh
Head of Product 
Stellar Payments


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <psingh@stellarpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 08:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: TTFT & streaming targets (recap + next steps)

Priya — perfect, that helps. Quick confirmation and proposed assignment: 
- Marking checkout/authorization as critical path: TTFT p99 <= 250 ms. Non-critical flows allowed higher p99. We will annotate the SLO_proposal file and highlight the critical route.
- Ben will deliver two harness variants (SSE + WebSocket) and report median/p95/p99 TTFT, initial-chunk timing, and chunk cadence. Expect: harness + initial results in 4 business days (target Tue, Apr 27).
- Laura will propose two pilot windows next week for the 2-day sandbox (we should aim for one that includes a weekday promo spike to observe realistic bursts).
- Security: attaching the SOC2 summary page in the shared Drive link; Ben, please note and be ready for the questionnaire.

Action items for Stellar (Priya / Prod Team): please upload the 200 anonymized samples to the Drive link and confirm which path you want to treat as critical (checkout confirmed).

Ben / Laura — can you confirm you have capacity for the timeline above and that the pilot-runbook.xlsx covers the burst windows and measurement points (TTFT, bytes to first chunk, chunk gap histogram)?

Thanks — Alex

On Thu, Apr 22, 2027 at 2:12 PM Priya Singh <psingh@stellarpay.com> wrote:
> Thanks Alex — good summary. A couple of clarifications from our side:...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Re: Post-demo: TTFT & streaming targets (recap + next steps)

- Measurement points: initial socket handshake, server ACK, first-byte delivered, first-token parse time, chunk intervals. I’ll include a small script to synthesize the 95th/99th percentiles.
- Env: US-west region, mock routing to fallback L7 pool to validate graceful-degradation policy.
- Timeline: start harness dev today (Ben), internal dry run Mon/Tue, deliver to Stellar Tue evening with sample results.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@novaops.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-19T13:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Scaling credit and target rate qualification

Elena — appreciate the quick details, this is great and gives us exactly what we need to model. A few concrete notes on how the programs stack and an initial set of scenarios.

How startup credits work:
- Qualifying startups receive a one-time onboarding credit (applied as an invoice credit) and an introductory effective rate for months 1–3. For deals that convert to committed-use, the onboarding credit reduces the first invoice(s) and we apply the committed discount from month 1 of the term.

Committed-use discount mechanics (high level):
- Discount is applied to per-token list prices based on annualized token commitment (tokens/year).
- Tiers (example):
  - Band A: 0–250M tokens/year -> baseline discount (5–10%)
  - Band B: 250–1,200M tokens/year -> mid discount (15–30%)
  - Band C: 1,200M+ tokens/year -> highest discount (30–45%)
- Multi-year: commit 24 months and we add a 5–10% term bonus on top of tier discount, or offer a rebate structure that pays out at year-end based on actual consumption vs commitment.

Initial scenarios (using NovaOps ramp and target $500/1M):
- Scenario 1 — 12mo commitment (annualized ~480M tokens/year based on conservative 40M/mo):
  - List unit price: $900 / 1M tokens (example list)
  - Mid-tier discount (25%) => $675 / 1M
  - Apply onboarding credit spread across first 3 months => effective year-1 $/1M ~= $620
  - Startup credit can reduce first-quarter cashflow by ~$20k (example)

- Scenario 2 — 24mo commitment (same year-1 consumption):
  - Mid-tier discount (25%) + term bonus (7%) => effective discount ~32% => $612 / 1M
  - Possible year-end rebate if consumption exceeds committed bands (improves effective rate further)

Notes: these are illustrative — we’ll run exact math in the spreadsheet attached (pricing-scenarios.xlsx) using your provided usage snapshot. We can also show a higher commitment plan if NovaOps is comfortable locking in a larger floor (e.g., 1,000M+/yr) which materially reduces $/1M.

Next steps I suggest:
1) Jonas will send the tailored workbook with the two scenarios filled in (includes gross $/1M, discount stack, credits, and net-first-year $/1M).
2) You share preferred term (12 vs 24) and any procurement constraints we should know about.
3) If positive, we introduce legal to get a term sheet + SOW and copy security for SOC2 evidence pack.

Attaching the scenarios workbook now. If you prefer, we can run a 30-min pricing review with finance next week to walk through the math live.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing-scenarios.xlsx (12-tab workbook: assumptions, 12mo/24mo math, rebate models)

title: Clarify where prompts & responses are stored by default — residency/retention
preview:
From: Daniel Santos <d.santos@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Hi Aisha,

Quick procurement check ahead of our contract review: can you confirm what Redwood stores by default when we make API calls to the hosted inference endpoint?

Specifically:
- Are prompts retained, or are only model responses kept?
- What is the default retention period for request/response logs (prompts, outputs, metadata)?
- Where do those logs live geographically for a US customer — are they stored in a region or multi-region?
- Can we opt out entirely or request on-demand deletion? We need exact language for our security questionnaire and the SOW.

Appreciate a short, precise answer and any supporting artifacts we can attach to our procurement package.

Thanks,
Daniel
Director, IT Security
Acme Health



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
To: Daniel Santos <d.santos@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodai.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodai.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Hi Daniel — thanks for the clear list. Short answers below; attaching a SOC2 summary and a retention matrix that spells out our defaults and options.

- Prompts vs outputs: For Hosted API (Redwood API), prompts (the request payload) and the model outputs (responses) are both considered request/response artifacts and are stored by default. We treat the prompt as part of the request record.

- Default retention period (Hosted): 45 days for request/response artifacts (this includes prompt text, outputs, and standard request metadata such as timestamps and model id). That 45-day period is our default for non-enterprise self-serve customers; it supports debugging, quality monitoring, and safety tooling.

- Residency (Hosted US): Logs are persisted within our US region (primary: us-west-2), and are not replicated to EU/APAC for US-region customers unless configured via contract. We can provide region-specific attestations.

- Opt-outs / deletion: We support on-demand deletion requests via our Data Operations API and manual deletion requests through your account team. For Hosted customers the default is retention → deletion after 45 days; we can process expedited deletion requests (within 72 hours) under agreed workflows. For Dedicated and Private deployments, retention policies are configurable by the customer (see next point).

Attachments sent: SOC2_summary.pdf (attestation excerpt), retention_matrix.csv (breakdown by deployment mode & artifact type).

If you need formal contract language, I can loop in Rishi (security) and Ben (sales ops) to propose SOW wording.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: SOC2_summary.pdf (stub), retention_matrix.csv (stub)


From: Daniel Santos <d.santos@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Sat, 17 Apr 2027 08:41:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Thanks — that's helpful. A couple of follow-ups from procurement/security: 

1) We need exact contract language that guarantees US residency for logs and a deletion SLA (ideally 72 hours for expedited deletes). Can that be in the SOW?  
2) Is there a way to prevent Redwood from storing prompts at all (i.e., turn off prompt retention) for hosted customers? If not, is hashing/partial redaction an option so PII isn't stored in plain text?
3) For Dedicated/Private: confirm that retention is customer-configurable and whether Redwood can automate purge on a schedule (e.g., 7 days, 30 days, 90 days) — procurement wants to know whether the purge can be enforced by policy in the stack.

Would prefer proposed SOW language in plain text.

-Daniel


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodai.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodai.com>
Date: Sat, 17 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Looping you both in — Acme Health is asking for SOW-friendly language and a deletion SLA. Draft suggested language below that we can use in SOW/contract negotiations. Ben — can you quote a 72-hour expedited deletion SLA as an optional add-on? Rishi — any security redlines we should avoid?

Proposed SOW language (draft):

"Data residency and default retention: For Redwood-hosted API services, Customer data including request payloads (prompts) and model outputs (responses) are retained by Redwood for a standard period of 45 days to support service debugging, safety monitoring, and quality telemetry. For customers requiring specific residency, Redwood will store Customer data in the geographic region specified in the Order Form (for Acme Health: US-West region)."

"Deletion and expedited deletion: Redwood will implement deletion of Customer request/response artifacts in accordance with the Customer's request. Standard deletion following the default retention period occurs automatically after 45 days. Upon receipt of an expedited deletion request, Redwood will confirm receipt within 24 hours and complete deletion from active logs within 72 hours, excluding backups and archival snapshots described in the backup policy (see SOC2_summary.pdf)."

"Dedicated and Private deployments: For Dedicated and Private deployments, retention periods and automatic purge schedules are configurable by the Customer and may be enforced as a policy on the deployment. Redwood will provide automation to support scheduled purges (examples: 7, 30, 90 days) and integration points for Customer key-management and audit logging."  

Other points to consider for Acme procurement:
- Offer optional prompt-hashing or on-the-fly redaction for hosted customers as a paid feature — reduces storing full prompt text.
- Note: backups and archives may be retained longer per backup retention schedule; expedited deletion will remove primary records and cached copies but will follow archival recovery/retention rules in the backup policy.

Attachments: redline_suggestion.docx (draft clause + a small redline)

If that looks good I'll send the draft back to Daniel and include the SOC2 excerpt and retention matrix.

Thanks,
Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay - hosted API
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Chen <emily@ezpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I put together a lightweight proposal that tries to balance EzPay's ask for predictability and your desire to capture upside. The attached PDF (EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf) contains the full table; below is the executive summary and a suggested mutual action plan.

Executive summary
- Baseline per-token list rate: $0.00045 (hosted API, text-generation)
- Anchored breakpoints (quarterly aggregated):
  * Tier A: 0 - 50M tokens -> baseline rate
  * Tier B: 50M - 200M tokens -> marginal rebate 12%
  * Tier C: 200M+ tokens -> marginal rebate 22%
- Transitional smoothing credit: for Q1 post-commit, we apply a retroactive blended credit up to 6% if you ramp slower than forecast (protects you vs. step cliffs)
- Overage treatment: marginal overage rebate capped at 15% (to avoid runaway discounts)

Why this structure? It preserves marginal economics (you only get the higher rebate for incremental tokens beyond a breakpoint) while adding an anchored smoothing credit so billing is readable for your finance team. This is a middle ground between pure blended and pure marginal approaches.

Next steps / asks
1) Internal confirmation: can procurement accept retroactive credits applied as invoice adjustments?
2) Would EzPay prefer rebates applied retroactively (credit back on invoice) or as upfront monthly bill-credit?
3) Can we aim for an LOI signature within the next two weeks to lock pricing for calendar Q3?

Proposed mutual action plan (high-level)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Agree with the three-option breakdown. My recommendation (and the one I think minimizes ops friction) is Option B with a small tweak: quarterly true-up combined with a one-time ramp credit that phases out after Q1. Practical details below and I attached a working model (pricing_model_sheet.xlsx).

Recommended approach (Option B, detailed)
- Monthly invoice: apply a predictable floor blended rate equal to a conservative forecasted effective rate (e.g., $0.00042/token for Q1).
- Quarterly true-up: after quarter close, compute actual marginal rebates retroactively and apply as an invoice credit. This preserves marginal economics while keeping monthly invoices simple.
- One-time ramp credit: if actual Q1 usage < forecast by >20%, apply a smoothing credit up to 6% of billed amount (not to exceed $50k) to avoid an early churn risk.
- Overage: treat as incremental tokens above the top tier; rebate applied at marginal rates but capped at 15% (we can negotiate higher cap for higher commits).
- Term flexibility: 12-month initial commitment with a mid-term review at 6 months; thresholds can be adjusted by mutual consent if usage deviates >40% vs forecast.

Sample numbers (see attached workbook for math)
- Forecast: 120M tokens / quarter
- Floor blended monthly: $0.00042/token -> monthly invoice simplicity
- Quarterly retroactive calc: if actual tokens hit tier C, effective retroactive rebate increases effective rate by ~10-15% on marginal tokens

Impacts to Redwood: minor complexity in running quarterly true-ups (we already do this for several book-of-business deals). Finance ops: we should document the crediting mechanism clearly (invoice line item: "Quarterly rebate adjustment - EZPay").

If you want, I can draft the one-page summary (blurb + two examples) and Aisha can send it to Emily. Recommend we include the workbook as a supplement.

Thanks,
Ben Carter

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Chen <emily@ezpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I put together a lightweight proposal that tries to balance EzPay's ask for predictability and your desire to capture upside. The attached PDF (EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf) contains the full table; below is the executive summary and a suggested mutual action plan.

Executive summary
- Baseline per-token list rate: $0.00045 (hosted API, text-generation)
- Anchored breakpoints (quarterly aggregated):
  * Tier A: 0 - 50M tokens -> baseline rate
  * Tier B: 50M - 200M tokens -> marginal rebate 12%
  * Tier C: 200M+ tokens -> marginal rebate 22%
- Transitional smoothing credit: for Q1 post-commit, we apply a retroactive blended credit up to 6% if you ramp slower than forecast (protects you vs. step cliffs)
- Overage treatment: marginal overage rebate capped at 15% (to avoid runaway discounts)

Why this structure? It preserves marginal economics (you only get the higher rebate for incremental tokens beyond a breakpoint) while adding an anchored smoothing credit so billing is readable for your finance team. This is a middle ground between pure blended and pure marginal approaches.

Next steps / asks
1) Internal confirmation: can procurement accept retroactive credits applied as invoice adjustments?
2) Would EzPay prefer rebates applied retroactively (credit back on invoice) or as upfront monthly bill-credit?
3) Can we aim for an LOI signature within the next two weeks to lock pricing for calendar Q3?

Proposed mutual action plan (high-level)
- Week 0: Aisha shares v1 proposal (attached)
- Week 1: Procurement review & confirm billing approach; Redwood legal to prepare LOI
- Week 2: Sign LOI; schedule technical kickoff

Attaching: EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf with the breakpoint tables and examples. Happy to hop on a short call to walk through the math.

Best,

Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
<aisha@redwood.inference>

title: SummitCare: temporary quarterly invoicing + net-60 request
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dana Morales <accounts-payable@summitcare.org>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: quarterly invoicing pilot + net-60 (SummitCare)

Hi Dana —

Thanks again for the thoughtful conversation this morning. Per SummitCare finance, they want to move to a quarterly invoicing cadence aligned to their internal consolidation dates for the next two quarters (May and August invoices). Their ask:

- Quarterly invoices (one invoice covering the quarter) starting 2027-05-01 for Q2 and Q3 as a pilot.
- Net-60 payment terms from invoice date (our standard is Net-30).
- If Redwood accepts quarterly invoices, SummitCare would prepay 30% of each quarterly invoice within 7 days of receipt; they are also requesting a 10% early-payment credit on prepaid amount as an incentive.

They framed this as a temporary cashflow alignment while they consolidate AP systems; this is not intended to be permanent. Their AR contact is Dana Morales (accounts-payable@summitcare.org). Total expected quarterly invoice is ~ $180k (variable by usage).

Attached is a proposed AP schedule they sent (SummitCare_AP_Schedule_v1.xlsx) and a short MOU they asked us to sign (MOU_quarterly-proposal.pdf).

I want to surface this for exec/finance approval because it deviates from our standard Net-30 and invoicing cadence. Key commercial asks for approval:

1) Allow Net-60 for two quarters only (Q2 & Q3 2027).
2) Approve quarterly invoicing for the pilot window, with Redwood to invoice on the 1st of the month following quarter close.
3) Approve 10% credit on the 30% prepaid portion (applies only to prepaid amount).
4) Require escrow or dedicated remittance instructions for > $250k exposure (finance request).

Can someone from Finance + Deal Desk confirm risk tolerance and whether we can approve a conditional MOU? If we agree, I’ll send a short counter-MOU and convert to an order form.

Appreciate rapid turnaround — customer hopes to start May 1.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: SummitCare_AP_Schedule_v1.xlsx (xlsx)]
[Attachment: MOU_quarterly-proposal.pdf (pdf)]

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly invoicing pilot + net-60 (SummitCare)

Ben — quick internal summary / risk note before we escalate: 

- Customer history: SummitCare is an existing customer (since 2025) on standard monthly billing. Usage has grown ~20% q/q; ARR footprint ~ $600k.
- Risk: moving to quarterly invoicing increases exposure window significantly; Net-60 compounds that. If they prepay 30% that mitigates but does not eliminate consumption risk.
- Operational impact: quarterly invoices require PSR change and a different invoice identifier for our billing system; ops says 5 business days to implement once approved.
- Suggested mitigations we recommend to leadership:
  * Limit pilot to two quarters (Q2 & Q3 2027), automatic reversion to monthly afterward.
  * Require 30% prepayment as stated, funds cleared before license/overage invoicing considered satisfied.
  * Cap total Redwood exposure at $300k outstanding — any usage above is invoiced monthly.
  * Approve a 10% credit only applied to the prepaid portion, not to the net outstanding balance.
  * Use escrow or dedicated remittance instructions for amounts > $250k — legal to draft simple addendum.

If leadership is comfortable, we should accept conditionally and sign a 2-page addendum that covers: term, restart to monthly, escrow clause, and the early-pay credit mechanics.

—Monica
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Apr 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Request: quarterly invoicing pilot + net-60 (SummitCare) — formal approval requested

Jonas / Marissa — forwarding the ask and Monica’s recommendations. Customer needs a decision this week so they can align treasury for May 1 start. My commercial ask summarized: 

Proposal for approval (one-liner): Redwood to accept temporary quarterly invoicing + Net-60 for two quarters, conditioned on 30% prepayment, a cap on outstanding exposure, and a 10% credit applied only to advance/prepaid portion.

Detailed terms we need to approve (for Deal Desk signoff):
1) Term: Pilot limited to Q2 & Q3 2027, auto-revert to monthly unless both parties agree in writing.
2) Payment: Quarterly invoice sent 1st business day after quarter close; Net-60 on invoice date. 30% of each invoice to be prepaid within 7 days; unpaid prepaid portion voids credit.
3) Credit: 10% early-payment credit only applied to the prepaid 30% amount (not to total invoice).
4) Exposure/cap: Redwood exposure cannot exceed $300k undisputed outstanding. If usage would push beyond $300k, additional charges billed monthly.
5) Security of funds: For invoices > $250k, funds must be held in escrow or via approved remittance account until cleared.
6) Legal addendum: Legal to issue a 1–2 page addendum capturing the above; no changes to core T&Cs except invoicing & payment mechanics.

Why approve? Customer retention risk is moderate — they are evaluating alternatives for a specific product line. Commercial upside: potential increase in usage and expansion next renewal.

Ask: please indicate: 
- Approve as-is (Jonas/Marissa)
- Approve with edits (please call out edits)
- Decline (we’ll propose alternate mitigations to customer)

If approved, I’ll draft the addendum and route for e-signature.

Thanks,
Ben
[Quoted original message below]

On Wed, 7 Apr 2027 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Dana —
>
> Thanks again for the thoughtful conversation this morning...
> [Attachment: SummitCare_AP_Schedule_v1.xlsx]

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 11 Apr 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: quarterly invoicing pilot + net-60 (SummitCare) — formal approval requested

Ben / Marissa — after quick review with Finance, we can approve this as a limited pilot with the following mandatory conditions (Finance & Legal):

1) Pilot limited to Q2 & Q3 2027 (auto-revert to monthly) — APPROVED.
2) 30% prepayment must be cleared in our account before the quarter is considered paid; we will not extend services withheld for non-payment but will flag usage for potential suspension if exposure > $300k — APPROVED.
3) 10% early-payment credit may be offered but it must be capped: credit applies only to the prepaid 30% and maximum credit per quarter capped at $5,000 (to limit precedent).
4) Exposure: total outstanding undisputed exposure capped at $300k — APPROVED.
5) Escrow: for any single quarterly invoice > $250k, require funds in an approved escrow account or wire confirmation; Legal to prepare a short addendum capturing remittance mechanics.
6) Accounting/Revenue recognition: RevRec to confirm but Finance flags that the 30% prepay will be treated as deferred revenue until services are delivered; ensure Accounting signs off before sending the addendum.

Jonas asks Legal to draft a one-page addendum capturing the above; Deal Desk: once Legal posts the addendum to the drive link, Ben can send to SummitCare for signature.

Decision: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL (Finance + Legal conditions above).

Jonas Weber
Head of Finance, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Received and noted by Deal Desk; Ben to proceed to finalize addendum and return to customer.

Detailed terms we need to approve (for Deal Desk signoff):
1) Term: Pilot limited to Q2 & Q3 2027, auto-revert to monthly unless both parties agree in writing.
2) Payment: Quarterly invoice sent 1st business day after quarter close; Net-60 on invoice date. 30% of each invoice to be prepaid within 7 days; unpaid prepaid portion voids credit.
3) Credit: 10% early-payment credit only applied to the prepaid 30% amount (not to total invoice).
4) Exposure/cap: Redwood exposure cannot exceed $300k undisputed outstanding. If usage would push beyond $300k, additional charges billed monthly.
5) Security of funds: For invoices > $250k, funds must be held in escrow or via approved remittance account until cleared.
6) Legal addendum: Legal to issue a 1–2 page addendum capturing the above; no changes to core T&Cs except invoicing & payment mechanics.

Why approve? Customer retention risk is moderate — they are evaluating alternatives for a specific product line. Commercial upside: potential increase in usage and expansion next renewal.

Ask: please indicate: 
- Approve as-is (Jonas/Marissa)
- Approve with edits (please call out edits)
- Decline (we’ll propose alternate mitigations to customer)

If approved, I’ll draft the addendum and route for e-signature.

Thanks,
Ben

Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the quick demo last week — the team at Lighthouse Labs is excited about how Redwood could speed up our inferencing pipeline. We kicked off an internal pilot and hit a usage spike this month that pushed us past the free trial cap. Procurement is asking for a combined ask: some emergency credits to keep the pilot live plus a short-term commercial discount if we sign to a 12-month commitment.

Quick numbers:
- Estimated extra consumption this month: ~450k tokens (spike tied to a new customer beta)
- Pilot -> Expected production ARR if we convert: $250k ARR conservatively
- Procurement timeline: we can sign MSAs within ~4 weeks if terms are nailed down

What we're requesting:
1) A one-time sponsorship credit to cover the current surge (~$10k–$25k depending on how you size credits) to avoid throttling the pilot.
2) A promotional discount (they are asking ~30% off list) applied to the first 12 months tied to a signed commitment.

I attached a short proposal and an export of usage for the last six weeks. Legal already flagged standard terms but procurement is willing to accept a shorter review window if your team can provide a clean term-sheet.

Can we escalate for exec approval? This is timing sensitive for the signed commitment.

Best,
Emma
Emma Diaz
Head of Product, Lighthouse Labs
-- attachments: sponsorship_proposal.pdf; lighthouse_usage_export.csv

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount

Marissa, Ben — forwarding Lighthouse's ask. TL;DR: potential $250k ARR if they convert; pilot hit a usage surge and procurement wants credits + a 12-month promotional discount. Attachments from them included (see below).

My recommendation (from SDR/AE view):
- Offer a $20k one-time sponsorship credit to cover the immediate spike (covers current overage + buffer).
- Offer a 25% promotional discount on list pricing for the first 12 months in exchange for a signed 12-month commitment and a short NRR check at 6 months.
- Require Legal review and inclusion of a usage-based cap clause (to avoid open-ended subsidized overages).

Rationale: Lighthouse is engineering-led, has strong technical fit, and is already integrating our SDK in two internal services — high chance of expansion across their product lines. If we decline or move slowly, they will evaluate other vendors next week.

Attachments: redwood_term_sheet_v2.docx (draft term summary I can push to procurement once you greenlight).

Ask: need exec approval for the combined credit + discount (this is outside standard field authority). If you want different numbers, I can adjust.

—Aditya

- Alternative: keep $20k credit but require a 12-month commit + a 6-month review gate tied to specific usage milestones. If they miss the milestone, discount steps down.

title: Request to exclude a single conversation from retention reporting (LUM-83)
preview:
From: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@lumenhealth.com
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Hi Alex,

We discovered that a sensitive thread containing patient-identifiable PII was inadvertently retained in your audit/retention reporting for one of our integrations. Per our contract and data residency policy, we need Redwood to remove/omit that single conversation ID from all analytics/retention exports and confirm the removal for our compliance audit.

Details: 
- Account: Lumen Health
- Conversation ID (vendor side): LUM-83
- Approximate date of original conversation: 2027-02-18

Can you confirm whether Redwood can:
1) Exclude that conversation from any aggregated reporting/exports and from retention indexes going forward,
2) Provide a short redaction report showing where the item was found and what was removed, and
3) Confirm the action in writing for our auditors.

We'd like to close this by EOD Wed PT if possible. Happy to sign any attestation or DPA addendums you require.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Wong
Senior InfoSec Program Manager
Lumen Health
Attachment: sample-thread-id.txt (text/plain)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 09:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Hi Sarah — thanks for the heads up and the details. Looping in Lauren (CS) and Ben (Legal/Privacy) so we can coordinate the operational and compliance path. Quick clarifying Qs before we proceed:

1) Is the request limited to: analytics/retention exports and redaction from our audit indices, or do you also want the underlying storage object deleted from all backups and S3 buckets?
2) Do you have a signed legal hold or other retention exception that conflicts with deletion?

Operationally we can run a targeted redaction job against conversation ID LUM-83 and produce a CSV showing the log locations where we removed entries. Ben will advise on required attestation language. If you prefer, we can send a DocuSign attestation for signature to expedite.

I can target a completion estimate of 48 hours after confirmation of scope.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Support Lead, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
Attachment: internal-ticket-CASE-2027-4472 (ticket stub)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Thanks Alex / Sarah. Legal/Privacy here — short answer: yes, we can perform a targeted exclusion from reporting and produce a redaction report. A couple of items so we stay audit-safe: 

• Scope confirmation: Unless Lumen asks for full media/storage object deletion (including historical backups), we will perform an omission from analytics/retention indexes and remove any derived logs that feed reporting. Please confirm if full object deletion is required.
• Attestation: We need a signed attestation from the Lumen primary contact authorizing the operation and confirming no legal hold exists. I can draft a one-paragraph attestation and circulate via DocuSign.
• Timeline: Once we have signed attestation and conversation ID confirmed, engineering will schedule a redaction job in the next business window. Estimated completion: 24–48 hours, followed by a generated redaction_report CSV and a confirmation email.

Alex — please create CASE-2027-4472 and set priority to High. Lauren — please coordinate engineering window and deliverable formatting for the auditors. Sarah — if you can reply with either 1) confirmation that the scope above is sufficient, or 2) explicitly request full deletion from backups, we’ll proceed accordingly.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Sr. Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference


From: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@lumenhealth.com
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Ben / Alex — thanks. Lumen confirms scope: exclusion from analytics/retention indexes and removal from reporting exports is sufficient for our auditors; we do NOT require destruction from historical immutable backups at this time. We will sign the attestation. Please send the DocuSign link.

Sarah Wong
Senior InfoSec Program Manager, Lumen Health
Attachment: signed-POC-stub (PDF)

DocuSign envelope completed: docusign-lumen-LUM-83.pdf


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Wong <sarah.wong@lumenhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove / exclude a single conversation from retention reporting - LUM-83

Sarah — quick update and closure:

1) We received the signed DocuSign attestation and attached it to CASE-2027-4472 (see link).
2) Engineering ran a targeted redaction job against conversation ID LUM-83 during the 2027-03-31 maintenance window. The job omitted the conversation from analytics/retention indexes and removed derived reporting entries; backups were not altered per your confirmation.
3) Attached is the redaction report: redaction_report_LUM-83.csv — it lists the tables/indices and the timestamped actions.

Final confirmation: the conversation LUM-83 has been excluded from reporting exports and retention indexes. We are preserving an internal audit trail of the redaction action (as required by our policy) and have attached the auditor-friendly report.

If you need an expanded report format or a signed cover letter for your auditors, let us know and Ben will prepare it.

Thanks for working through this quickly — closing CASE-2027-4472 now.

title: Multi-cloud listing: splitflow & edge-region launch decisions
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Mar 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Multi-cloud listing: splitflow & edge-region launch decisions

Team —

title: Retroactive capacity backcharge — Jan spike and escrow proposal
preview:
From: Kyle Anders <kyle.anders@clearbridge.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Chen <maria.chen@clearbridge.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Retroactive capacity charge — Jan spike and renewal

Hi Aditya,

Quick note from procurement after we closed last week's renewal: our finance team flagged a +42% usage spike in January tied to a new ingestion pipeline we flipped on. It looks like that pushed us above our committed capacity for the month and produced an overage that wasn't accounted for in the renewal.

Can you share the line-item reconciled numbers and a proposed one-time charge? Our CFO wants either an amortized settlement across Q1 or a short escrow holdback while we validate logs (we'll need audit evidence).

Attached our procurement form for PO routing. Happy to hop on a quick call to walk through the numbers.

Thanks,
Kyle
Kyle Anders
Head of Infrastructure Operations
ClearBridge AI

Attachments: procurement_form.pdf


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Kyle Anders <kyle.anders@clearbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>

title: Zip SSO invite blocked by device clock skew
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@sundale.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier portal invite - acceptance failing

Hi Aditya,

We tried to accept the Zip supplier invite you sent yesterday but the page returns an error immediately after clicking the accept button: "Invite token invalid (clock skew)". It looks like an auth check is rejecting the token on our side.

I attached the client-side trace (trace-log.txt) and a quick screenshot. Our IT team confirmed the user's phone was a corporate-managed Android (v13) with automatic time enabled — I can double-check the device clock if that helps.

Can you resend or provide an alternate link? We need to finish onboarding so we can receive the first PO from Redwood next week.

Thanks,
Liam
Liam O'Connor
IT Operations, Sundale Manufacturing
liam.oconnor@sundale.com
+1 415-555-0192

Attachment: trace-log.txt (text)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@sundale.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier portal invite - acceptance failing

Thanks for the debug log, Liam — sorry for the friction here.

Priyom / Ben — can you take a look? Vendor reports an "clock skew" error during Zip SSO acceptance. Trace suggests the client presents an iat/exp outside the tolerance window. Quick ideas:
- Is Zip configured with a strict 5m validation window for the invite token?
- Can we issue a one-off invite that extends TTL to 30m or uses a different HMAC seed?
- If not, can we provide a direct enrollment link (no SSO) as a temporary bypass?

Liam — we can try a resend with a longer expiration while we investigate. If you can share the exact timestamp from the device (or confirm NTP status) that would help pinpoint whether it's device-side drift or a token TTL issue.

Attaching the current invite stub (invite_link.html) for reference.

-Aditya

Attachment: invite_link.html (HTML stub)

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 13:27:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: trace-log.txt / analysis

Forwarding the relevant bits from our infra colleague (cut for brevity) — quote from the auth service trace: 

"[2027-02-20T16:09:12Z] auth.token.validate: token.exp=1677001752 token.iat=1677001452 now=1677001212 skew=240
error=EXP_BEFORE_ISSUE, signature_ok=true"

Interpretation: the vendor's client clock appears ~240s ahead of our server 'now' value which pushes the token verification into the fail path under the current 5-minute allowed skew. Zip's SSO library enforces iat/exp with default +/- 300s, but our invite generation sets a conservative 5-minute TTL and rejects tokens where iat > now + 30s (defense against replay). That combination can produce this exact rejection when NTP hasn't synchronized.

Workarounds:
- Short term: resend invite with TTL bumped to 30m and relax the iat future tolerance to 2 minutes for invite paths only (INF-8921).
- Medium term: add client-guidance to the invite email that instructs users to enable automatic time or check device settings; add a pre-check on accept that surfaces a friendly error with steps.
- Long term: make invite acceptance tolerant to reasonable drift by comparing server time to STS or using monotonic counters.

I'll file INF-8921 and propose the one-off TTL bump. Ben, can you issue the extended invite and confirm link generation parameters?

— Priyom

Related: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-8921

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@sundale.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Feb 2027 09:00:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier portal invite - extended TTL sent

Hi all,

I generated a new Zip invite and extended the acceptance TTL to 30 minutes. I also toggled the invite validation path to allow a 2-minute future-iat tolerance for invite acceptance only — this is a scoped, temporary change that will be rolled back after the vendor completes onboarding. The new link is attached (invite_link_extended.html) and I included acceptance steps in the email body below.

Liam — steps for Sundale:
1) Click the attached link from the device you will use to accept (mobile or desktop).
2) If you still see "clock skew" errors, please check device time settings and toggle "Automatic date & time" off then on again, restart the browser and retry.
3) If acceptance succeeds, reply here and we'll mark the account active and push the first PO.

If the issue persists, we'll temporarily issue a non-SSO enrollment code and complete a manual verification.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Operations Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachment: invite_link_extended.html (HTML stub)

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@sundale.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 21 Feb 2027 11:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier portal invite - extended TTL sent

Ben / Aditya,

That worked — the extended link allowed the user to accept and complete the profile. Our desktop had an industry VPN that was rewriting NTP packets; once we tested on a non-VPN device acceptance completed immediately.

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the clear steps. Please keep INF-8921 open if you plan a permanent fix; we'll test again if you roll the change back.

Best,
Liam O'Connor
IT Operations, Sundale Manufacturing
liam.oconnor@sundale.com
+1 415-555-0192

title: MAP: GPU split-test & multipass perf results + close timeline
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 10 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP draft: perf gating + hardware split-test proposal

Samantha — attaching our proposed Mutual Action Plan (high level) and the GPU split-test design so we can align on success gates. Key asks: sign off on the MAP milestones below and grant access for our evaluation harness to Polaris staging workload for 48 hours starting Jan 18.

High-level MAP (milestones):
- Jan 12: Confirm evaluation seeds (prompts + sample traffic) — Polaris
- Jan 18–19: Redwood executes split-test (A100 / H100 / L40S + MIG variants) on Polaris staging traffic
- Jan 20: Provide raw perf CSV + condensed results to Polaris
- Jan 22: Joint review and recommended fleet sizing + commercial proposal
- Jan 25: Finalize contract signature / reserved capacity start date

Perf success gates (we propose):
- TTFT (time-to-first-token) <= 70ms for single-stream unbatched at target model size
- p95 latency <= 200ms for 512-token responses at target concurrency
- sustained throughput >= 60k tokens/sec for 1-min steady-state on production mix
- graceful degradation (fallback to cheaper variant < 5% error rate)

Split-test design: run identical request stream with 1) H100 full, 2) A100 full, 3) L40S full, and 4) representative MIG slices (1g/2g equivalent). Each run: 2 warm-up minutes + 30 minutes steady-state; repeated twice. Instrument: p50/p95/p99 latency, TTFT, tokens/sec, GPU utilization, memory tail behavior, KV cache hit rates.

Attachments: polaris_map_v1.pdf (MAP), h100_a100_l40s_split_results.csv (template), ttft_concurrency_notes.pdf (test recipe)

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 11:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP draft: perf gating + hardware split-test proposal

Agree with the gates; one add: include a cold-start TTFT measurement after a 30-min idle window to simulate on-demand infrequent flows. Also propose adding a recommended cost-per-token delta in the results table to help procurement.

Quick checklist for the run engineers:
- Ensure KV cache priming scripts run prior to each steady-state window
- Use same model checkpoint across HW types (quantization profile applied consistently)
- Capture kernel selection logs for H100/A100 (useful for regressions)

I'll slot a canary in Production for Feb 1 if we clear the MAP by Jan 25.

- Karthik


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@cloudops.io>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jan 2027 08:32:00 -0800
Subject: Polaris staging access + eval harness instructions (Jan 18–19)

Samantha — thanks for the quick sync. Per our call:
- We've provisioned the Redwood eval harness and need a service account with read-only streaming access to Polaris staging Canary API.
- Start window: 2027-01-18 10:00 PST; duration: 48 hours, but we will only perform the split-test during the 10:00–14:00 PST window on Jan 18 and repeat Jan 19 if needed.

What to expect in the payload:
- 60/40 user:system distribution; avg request length ~ 420 tokens; 15% long-form (>1024 tokens)
- We will pin the same model (r-infer-2.1) and run the kernel selection for each HW type; quantization: 4-bit canonical profile.

Please upload the service-account key to the Drive folder and let us know when the staging permissions are live. We'll stream logs and attach the CSV results to the shared Drive link.

Aditya


From: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@cloudops.io>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 15:55:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Polaris staging access + eval harness instructions (Jan 18–19)

Team — staging key uploaded and access granted. We ran the first pass this morning; preliminary observations attached (short-form). A request: can Redwood include a MIG 3g slice in the second pass? Our ops team is considering both full-card and MIG economics.

Attached: polaris_staging_prelim_notes.txt (short), see Drive.

— Samantha


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Jan 2027 09:04:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: split-test condensed results + sizing recommendation (draft)

Team — pulling together the condensed results from the Jan 18 run (attached CSV). Top-line (steady-state windows, warmed KV cache):
- H100 (full): p50=7.8ms, p95=21.7ms, p99=45ms; avg throughput=122k tokens/sec; TTFT (single-stream, unbatched)=48ms
- L40S (full): p50=9.9ms, p95=31.2ms, p99=68ms; avg throughput=103k tokens/sec; TTFT=62ms
- A100 (full): p50=12.4ms, p95=39.8ms, p99=85ms; avg throughput=89k tokens/sec; TTFT=81ms
- MIG 3g slice (on H100): p50=18.6ms, p95=46.0ms, p99=110ms; avg throughput=38k tokens/sec; TTFT=95ms

Notes and interpretation:
- H100 wins on raw latency and throughput; L40S is close and offers a better price/throughput ratio for Polaris's mixed workload. A100 lags on p95/p99 and TTFT, which impacts interactive UX for short-turn flows.
- MIG slices are predictable for low-cost isolation but reduce throughput significantly; useful for small isolated tenant workloads.

Sizing recommendation (Polaris target: 300k tokens/sec steady-state + 200 concurrent interactive users):
- Preferred fleet: 6 x L40S (full) + 2 x H100 (full) for burst and low-latency tail coverage. This yields an expected steady throughput ~640k tokens/sec peak with headroom for autoscaling.
- Alternate cost-first fleet: 10 x L40S (full) and use MIG 3g slices for tenant isolation where needed.

Commercial delta: incremental cost per 1M tokens served (approx): H100 baseline: $11.2; L40S: $8.9; A100: $10.5 (approx; final quote in proposal).

Action items for MAP closure:
- Jan 22: Internal sign-off on sizing (this thread)
- Jan 24: Send draft commercial proposal to Polaris (Laura to lead)
- Jan 25: Final negotiation and DocuSign signatures

Ben
Senior Pricing Analyst


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Park <samantha.park@polarishealth.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Jan 2027 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Final MAP check-in: recommended fleet & next steps to close

Samantha — thanks for the partnership during the split-test. Summary and next steps: 

Summary (agreed):
- Performance: H100 best raw perf; L40S nearly as fast for steady workloads and presents the best cost/throughput trade-off for Polaris's workload mix. MIG slices are viable for tenant isolation but not for mainline throughput.
- Sizing: Recommended production fleet = 6 x L40S + 2 x H100; alternate cost-first proposal included in the draft commercial.
- Gates met: TTFT median and p95 targets met on warmed KV cache; cold-start TTFT failed the 70ms gate on A100 but passed on H100/L40S.

Next steps (timeline):
- Jan 25: Redwood sends final commercial (Laura) + DocuSign (done, link above)
- Jan 27: Polaris countersign (target)
- Feb 1: Redwood canary (small pct of traffic) if signatures in place
- Feb 7: Full capacity ramp on reserved fleet

Samantha — please confirm the counter-sign target and whether Polaris wants the alternative cost-first configuration included in the SOW. Once we have signatures we will schedule the Feb 1 canary and provide runbooks for rollback and autoscale thresholds.

Thanks everyone — moving to close.

Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
