title: MAP: Finvest evaluation gates & retention carveouts
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <security@finvest.com>
Date: 2027-01-04T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: MAP: Finvest evaluation gates & retention carveouts

Claire —

Thanks for taking the time yesterday. Per our conversation I've attached an initial Mutual Action Plan (Finvest-MAP-v1.xlsx) that maps evaluation checkpoints to deliverables and security gates. Top items we want to confirm with your team so we can finalize the MAP:

- SOC 2: you asked for Type II evidence — we can provide the latest Type II report and a scoped evidence bundle. Confirm target date for evidence review.
- SIG/CAIQ: we'll submit CAIQ responses and a redline-friendly workbook for your security engineers to annotate.
- DPA & retention: please confirm your retention window expectations (backups, audit logs, customer data) and any EU residency requirements.
- Subprocessors & pen test: we'll share the current subprocessors list and propose a pen-test contact.

Proposed close timeline (tentative):
- CAIQ workbook to Finvest: +10 business days (by 2027-01-14)
- Evidence bundle (SOC2 + configs): +20 business days (by 2027-01-26)
- DPA redlines loop complete & signature: target by 2027-02-14

Can you confirm the SOC 2 scope you need (production only vs production+staging) and whether EU residency is a hard requirement for the initial POC? I've cc'd Ben (technical lead) and Laura (legal) to keep them in the loop.

Attachments: Finvest-MAP-v1.xlsx

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha@redwood.com
--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference platform


From: Claire Huang <security@finvest.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Marco Rossi <legal@finvest.com>
Date: 2027-01-05T10:05:00-05:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Finvest evaluation gates & retention carveouts

Subject: Re: MAP: Finvest evaluation gates & retention carveouts

Thanks Claire — helpful notes. Short answers and proposed adjustments to the MAP (attached Finvest-MAP-v2.xlsx):

- SOC 2 scope: Redwood can include production + data-pipeline components in the evidence bundle. We'll mark any staging-only controls as out-of-scope and provide compensating evidence where needed.
- Retention tiers (proposed default, configurable per-account):
  * Session/temp caches: 30 days (configurable)

To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Claire Huang <security@finvest.com>
Date: 2027-01-08T09:40:00+01:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Finvest evaluation gates & retention carveouts

Aisha / Claire —

Reviewing the MAP v2 and the proposed retention tiers. A few legal redlines and questions attached (DPA-redlines-annotated.docx) — highlights below:

- EU residency clause: for any Finvest EU account we need an explicit Data Residency Addendum (Annex) that commits to processing within the EU for both primary data and backups.
- Retention: prefer 180 days for backups as default for Finvest; we can agree to 90 days for non-production. Please confirm whether backup replication to non-EU regions is avoidable when EU residency is required.
- Logs & fraud detection: Finvest needs the ability to retain certain raw logs for fraud analytics for up to 3 years; propose carveout language in the DPA with appropriate access controls and purpose limitation.
- Subprocessor notifications: require 30 days notice for new subprocessors and an option to review high-risk subprocessors.
- Pen test: please provide process for test scheduling, contact, and required approvals for external pentesters.

We will push back if EU residency for backups cannot be guaranteed — please confirm technical feasibility.

Regards,
Marco Rossi
General Counsel, Finvest Capital
legal@finvest.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: MAP: Finvest evaluation gates & retention carveouts

Marco / Claire —

Thanks for the redlines — technical confirmations below to support the DPA annex and MAP acceptance gates:

- EU residency: we support region pinning at the account level. When EU residency is selected we will ensure primary storage and automated backups remain in eu-central-1. Cross-region replication is disabled for accounts set to EU residency; manual exports remain possible under contract.
- Backup retention: default 90 days (enterprise option 180) — we can set 180 days as the default for Finvest under the enterprise terms you requested.
- Audit logs: stored 365 days in the logging cluster; we can provide export to a Finvest-owned archive for longer-term retention (up to 3 years) as a billable export service.
- Logs for fraud analytics: we can implement a scoped carveout with restricted access, purpose-limited retention, and encrypted-at-rest tagging. We'll include this language in the DPA annex.
- Subprocessors: attached Subprocessor-List-2027.csv. We will notify Finvest 30 days in advance for new subprocessors and provide expedited review for high-risk ones.
- Pen test: Redwood provides an onboarding packet and a security contact for scheduling (security-ops@redwood.com). We require a short authorization form; details in the packet.

Next steps:
- I'll upload a short data flow diagram and the CAIQ draft to the shared Drive by EOD tomorrow.
- Propose a 45-minute technical walkthrough on 2027-01-13 at 10:00am CET to go through CAIQ/SOC2 artifacts.
- Once Marco confirms the DPA annex language for EU residency & retention carveouts, we can exchange DocuSign URLs (target 2027-02-14).

Attachments: SOC2-TypeII-Report.pdf, Subprocessor-List-2027.csv

Ben Carter
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>

Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Commit signal review — flagged discount portfolios

Team,

Quick flag: pipeline sweep turned up a small set of deals where exceptions/discounts > 18% are still marked as committed in the forecast. I ran a quick heatmap (attached) and it shows concentration in two segments: Q4 pilots and a single enterprise account (BrightGrid).

Asks:
- Vanessa / Ben: can Deal Desk confirm which concessions already have L2 approvals and which are still pending?
- Marissa: finance wants a recommendation on whether we should continue to count these as committed for the December close or move to commit-at-risk.

I think we should consider a short-term hold on new discounts >20% until we align on approval cadence — that prevents last-minute forecast surprises. Proposed deadline for decisions: EOD Monday (12/14).

Attached: discount-heatmap-dec2026.csv (sensitivity by rep / stage)

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0182

Subject: Re: Commit signal review — flagged discount portfolios

On Thu, 10 Dec 2026 at 09:15, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
>

Thanks for the note — I pulled Deal Desk data and there are three active requests for BrightGrid (BG-47) tied to: pilot pricing, support credits, and a Fit & Onboard concession. Two have documented approvals from field leadership but none have final Deal Desk sign-off.

I'll run a single consolidated packet and drop it to Finance for Marissa to review. Short-term approach I recommend:
- Freeze any new >20% concessions from moving to \"Committed\" status until Deal Desk adjudicates.
- For deals currently in \"Committed\" state with >20% but without Deal Desk ticket, move to \"Commit-At-Risk\" and annotate reason in HubSpot.

I'll attach a compact exports pack to this thread in a follow-up.

Vanessa

Thanks — helpful summary. A few points from finance:

1) For the December close we cannot accept additional revenue we don't expect to cash. If Deal Desk cannot provide documented approvals, those line items should not be included in committed revenue.

2) For BrightGrid (RDW-2026-BG-47) — if the customer expects the discounted pilot to be billable in December we need an agreed acceptance date and signed SOW/PO. Otherwise move to Commit-At-Risk.

3) Practical policy: temporary threshold: discounts >20% must have a Deal Desk ticket and Deal Desk approval + signed commercial doc to be counted as committed.

Aditya: please send the customer-facing ask language to copy into the HubSpot comment so we can audit acceptance. Vanessa: please loop Ben for the ticket refs.

title: Contract-edit orchestra: roles, sprint dates, and redline queue
Procurement lane decision: Marketplace listing, Private Offer, or Direct Redwood contract
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@nimblehealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Marty Jacobs <marty.jacobs@nimblehealth.com>
Date: 2026-12-01T14:21:00Z
Subject: Procurement question — which purchase lane should we pick?

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Procurement is asking for a clear recommendation on whether we should buy Redwood through a cloud marketplace (AWS/Azure/GCP), request a Marketplace Private Offer, or go through a direct Redwood contract (MSA + PO). Their main concerns: speed of procurement, invoice and PO flow, discounts/credits, and any impact on support/SLA and co-sell benefits.

High-level context: Nimble Health is piloting a 2-month PoC (approx. $18k spend) and wants the option to scale to a reserved capacity commitment if things look good. The procurement team prefers marketplace buys for quick approval, but our legal team may request an MSA for anything above a 90-day pilot.

Can you outline pros/cons and practical timelines for each path? Also—any gotchas around co-sell credits or channel incentives we should be aware of?

Appreciate a short, actionable breakdown we can hand to procurement.

Thanks,
Samira
Samira Khan
Head of Procurement, Nimble Health
Samira.khan@nimblehealth.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

[Attachment stub: Nimble_SOW_draft.pdf]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Marty Jacobs <marty.jacobs@nimblehealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-02T09:07:00Z
Subject: Re: Procurement question — which purchase lane should we pick?

Samira — thanks for the clear context. Below is a concise, procurement-ready comparison and my recommendation based on Nimble's PoC -> scale profile. I’ve attached a short pricing matrix and a checklist we use for AWS Private Offers.

1) Cloud Marketplace (public listing)
- Speed: Fastest. Customer can purchase via existing cloud contract or consolidated invoice within 1–7 business days once procurement approves.
- Billing: Billed via the cloud provider’s invoice to the customer (consolidated cloud bill).
- Pricing & discounts: Standard list pricing on the listing. Limited ability to apply negotiated discounts — typically managed via Provider or Marketplace promotions, not line-item custom pricing.
- Support/SLA: Standard Redwood hosted SLA applies, but there are limits on contractual addenda via the marketplace. For pilots, this is often acceptable.
- Co-sell impact: Marketplace listing makes it straightforward for cloud partner teams to attribute usage, but co-sell incentive mechanics vary by partner and may require a private offer to capture special credits.
- Best when: Customer needs immediate procurement simplicity and is OK with standard pricing. Good for pilots and low-friction PoC purchases.

2) Marketplace Private Offer (MPP) / Cloud Private Offer
- Speed: Moderate. Setting up a private offer requires coordination between buyer procurement, cloud sales, and Redwood (usually 5–20 business days depending on approvals).
- Billing: Still billed through the cloud provider (customer sees charges on cloud bill), but pricing/discounts can be customized in the private offer.
- Pricing & discounts: Supports negotiated discounts, custom SKUs, and term-based commitments. Allows us to reflect a negotiated pilot price or scale pricing while keeping the billing path through the marketplace.
- Support/SLA: You can include a reference to the Redwood SLA, and commercial terms are captured in the private offer; however, legal teams sometimes still prefer an MSA for broader terms.
- Co-sell impact: Best path to preserve marketplace billing while enabling co-sell crediting and partner incentives — cloud partner needs to sign off on the private offer to make it co-sell-eligible.
- Best when: You want marketplace invoicing but need negotiated price/terms (pilot discounts, transition to commitment).

3) Direct Redwood Contract (MSA + PO / Redwood Invoice)
- Speed: Slowest. Negotiation of MSA and commercial terms typically 2–8 weeks (can be longer if custom legal terms).
- Billing: Redwood issues invoices directly (or can accept customer PO per the MSA).
- Pricing & discounts: Full flexibility to negotiate. Can include committed discounts, reserved capacity, and custom SLAs.
- Support/SLA: Full contract flexibility (dedicated support add-ons, explicit uptime targets, breach remedies).
- Co-sell impact: Co-sell crediting requires coordination; direct contracts can still be co-sell-enabled but may not flow through marketplace incentive systems automatically — we typically set a co-sell arrangement in parallel.
- Best when: Customer needs contractual protections, custom SLAs, or wants Redwood to be the invoicing party (finance or compliance reasons). Also recommended for long-term reserved capacity deals.

Recommendation for Nimble Health (PoC -> scale):
- For the 2-month PoC (~$18k) we recommend Marketplace public listing if procurement wants the fastest route and doesn’t need custom legal terms.
- If you want a discounted pilot price or anticipate rolling the pilot into a committed reserved capacity (and want to keep unified cloud billing), opt for a Marketplace Private Offer — it balances speed and negotiated pricing.
- If Nimble plans for immediate reserved capacity, data isolation, or custom SLAs, pursue a direct MSA and PO (longer timeline, more flexible commercial terms).

Next steps I propose:
- If you want to move quickly: Proceed via public marketplace (I can provide the listing link and PO guidance).
- If you want discount and cloud-billing continuity: I’ll open the Private Offer path and coordinate with the AWS account team (I’ll need the procurement contact and PO template).
- If legal needs an MSA first: I’ll send a draft MSA and our standard SOW (DocuSign link below).

Links & attachments: pricing matrix and AWS Private Offer checklist attached. DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/link/to/draft-msa

Happy to jump on a 20-min call with procurement/legal to walk their teams through each lane — what time tomorrow works?

Best,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0199
[Attachment stubs: Redwood_pricing_matrix_v3.pdf; AWS_private_offer_checklist.xlsx]

From: Marty Jacobs <marty.jacobs@nimblehealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Samira Khan <samira.khan@nimblehealth.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T11:32:00Z
Subject: Re: Procurement question — which purchase lane should we pick?

Thanks Aditya — that breakdown helps. A couple quick clarifications:
- If we do a private offer via AWS, does the invoice still flow through AWS and satisfy our existing PO process (i.e., we get a single consolidated cloud invoice)?
- For direct Redwood invoicing, can Redwood accept a standard Nimble PO and net-30 terms? We typically require PO + net-30 for vendor payments.
- Support: for pilots we usually accept standard support, but if we commit to reserved capacity we will want a defined escalation path and a named TAM. Is that something Redwood can include in the MSA/SOW?

If we can get quick answers to the above, procurement can confirm a preference.

—Marty

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Marty Jacobs <marty.jacobs@nimblehealth.com>, Samira Khan <samira.khan@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-05T17:48:00Z
Subject: Re: Procurement question — which purchase lane should we pick?

Marty — quick answers below and a recommended timeline: 

1) Private Offer invoicing: Yes — invoices are routed through AWS (or chosen cloud) and appear on your consolidated cloud bill. Private Offers allow custom pricing while keeping the cloud billing path intact. That satisfies most procurement PO reconciliation requirements.

2) Direct Redwood invoicing + PO: We can accept a Nimble PO and standard net-30 terms. We’ll include a payment clause in the MSA that aligns to PO processes. I’ve attached a draft MSA and SOW for your review (Redwood_MSA_draft_2026-12.docx).

3) Named TAM / support for reserved capacity: Yes — for committed/reserved customers we include a named Technical Account Manager and defined escalation SLAs in the SOW. We can scope response times and on-call windows in the MSA addendum.

Suggested timelines (typical):
- Public marketplace listing (purchase path ready): 1–7 business days from procurement approval.
- Marketplace Private Offer: 5–20 business days depending on cloud partner approvals and contract review.
- Direct MSA + PO (negotiation + signatures): 2–8 weeks depending on legal review depth.

If procurement wants to move fast, I recommend public marketplace for the PoC. If you want the pilot price locked and a clean transition to commitment while keeping your cloud billing, let me start the Private Offer workflow and I’ll loop in the AWS account team.

Next step: I’ll open the Private Offer intake if you confirm procurement contact and their PO template. Alternatively I can send the public listing link and a one-line PO sample for the quick path.

Best regards,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0199
[Attachments: Redwood_MSA_draft_2026-12.docx]

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@acme.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Commit confidence request + assumptions for Q4 run-rate

Hi Sarah —

Per our conversation last week I wanted to share the latest pipe snapshot and three asks so we can lock the Q4 commit. Attached: pipeline_snapshot_Q4.xlsx (summary tab + deal-level assumptions).

Key asks:
1) Confirm the decision date and procurement lead for the core contract (we have Nov 30 as target but need confirmation).
2) Flag any dependencies (security questionnaire, legal redlines, or integration POC) that would push timelines beyond 12/15.
3) Confirm who will own the PO and expected payment terms (Net30 / Net45).

Our current coverage math (net new ARR motion): 1.8x pipe-to-quota assuming 40% close rate for late-funnel deals and 75% for committed pilots. If any of those assumptions look off on your side, please call them out — we can adjust the rollout plan accordingly.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 15-min sync today if easier.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

title: Data residency & retention: hosted API — Q&A
preview:
From: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Aisha / Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week. Our security team is working through the vendor questionnaire and had a couple of targeted asks about Redwood's hosted API:

1) If we use the hosted API, can we ensure all customer data (requests & generated content) remains within US regions only?
2) What is the default retention window for request/response logs and model telemetry? Where can we find a formal purge process and SLA?
3) For compliance, they also want to know whether backups/snapshots might replicate data outside the requested region and how long those are retained.

Can you point me to a short, plain-language summary and any attachments we can pass to InfoSec? We're on a tight timeline for our vendor review.

Appreciate it —
Gina
Security & Privacy, Acme Tech

---


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Gina —

Thanks — great questions. Short answers up front, followed by a few operational details and attachments. I've CC'd Marcus (security) and Naomi (product ops) for technical backup.

1) Regional residency: Yes — when provisioning a hosted API key/account you can specify a US-only serving region (us-west-2 or us-east-1). All runtime request processing, ephemeral KV/prefix caches, and persistent logs for that tenant are stored in the selected region. We do not route request payloads to other regions for processing unless explicitly configured in routing policies.

2) Retention windows: The default retention for request/response logs and model telemetry is 30 days. Customers may request shorter windows (e.g., 7 days) as part of their onboarding. We also offer a paid retention-extension option (90/365 days) for audit needs. See attached retention-sla-summary.pdf for the table and options.

3) Backups & replication: Regular backups are taken for control-plane metadata and deployment state. Customer request/response content is excluded from cross-region backups by default for region-locked tenants. For Dedicated or Private deployments we provide stronger guarantees; hosted customers can opt into dedicated snapshots (US-only) if needed.

I've attached: data-residency-faq.pdf (plain-language summary), retention-sla-summary.pdf (SLA table + purge timelines), and a redwood-soc2-evidence.zip stub containing pointers to our evidence artifacts.

Action items:
- If you want an auditable statement we can provide a one-page attestation that we will keep Acme data in US regions for your tenant — Ben, can you pick this up and route a doc (simple letter) to Gina?
- Marcus/Naomi: can you confirm the backup snapshot cadence and retention for hosted US-only tenants so I can call it out explicitly?

If helpful we can also schedule a 30-min call with InfoSec next week.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: data-residency-faq.pdf (PDF), retention-sla-summary.pdf (PDF), redwood-soc2-evidence.zip (ZIP)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 14:37:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Gina — quick note from the sales side plus operational details Marcus provided: 

• Transient artifacts: The inference runtime uses ephemeral instance-local storage for short-lived caches (KV/prefix cache) which are zeroed on process termination. For US-region tenants we do not replicate instance-local ephemeral disks cross-region. Networked metrics/telemetry are aggregated to our metrics cluster in-region, then rolled up; raw request/response content is not used for metric aggregation.

• Deletion workflow: We have an automated retention worker that runs hourly to mark records past their retention window and a daily background job that purges marked records. Tombstones (small metadata records indicating a purge occurred) are retained for 7 days to support eventual consistency reconciliation only; they do not contain customer request/response payloads. Audit hooks: every purge run emits a signed audit event to the customer's audit log (stored in-region) and to our internal audit pipeline (SOC2-covered). See retention-sla-summary.pdf section 3 for the job cadence and retention of purge metadata.

• DPA clause: We can provide a DPA snippet specifying US-only processing and an obligation to disclose any cross-region transfers in advance; I'll attach a draft clause to the HubSpot deal and send you the Doc we discussed.

Next steps:
- I uploaded the attestation draft and the DPA snippet to the HubSpot deal and the Drive folder (links above).
- If Acme wants an on-record session, Marcus is available Wed/Thu for a 30-min technical deep-dive.

If you want me to convert the deletion workflow into a one-page diagram for your audit packet, say the word and we'll produce that today.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

title: Re: CPRA addendum — telemetry handling & subprocessor onboarding
preview:
From: Claire Morris <claire.morris@big-retail.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Team <legal@big-retail.com>, procurement@big-retail.com
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: CPRA addendum — telemetry + subprocessors

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the demo last week — Big Retail is moving fast on the pilot. Our privacy team has reviewed Redwood's DPA and asked for a short CPRA addendum to explicitly confirm:

- Redwood's classification as a \"service provider\" under CPRA/CPRA;
- How telemetry (system logs, usage metrics, aggregated analytics) is handled and retained;
- The process and timing for adding new subprocessors and how we receive notice; and
- Whether any telemetry could be used to re-identify customer end users.

I've attached a draft addendum with suggested redlines. Can you confirm who on your side will take point and whether Redwood can accept the short notice period we requested (10 business days) for new subprocessors?

Claire Morris
Senior Counsel — Privacy
Big Retail Co

Attachment: CPRA_Addendum_Draft_v1.docx

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Claire Morris <claire.morris@big-retail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: CPRA addendum — telemetry + subprocessors

Claire —

Appreciate the clear summary and the draft. Short answers up front: we are comfortable with the \"service provider\" classification in the CPRA context; telemetry is collected for operational purposes and is not sold or used to build new products for other customers; and we can commit to a subprocessors notice cadence, but the notice period is something we'd like to align on.

A few specifics we'll propose in our redline:
- Telemetry scope: \"system logs, aggregated usage metrics, latency and error telemetry, operational traces; excludes PII & content of customer-managed data flows unless expressly consented.\"
- Retention: default 90 days for most telemetry; 365 days for security logs (audit, incident investigations) unless otherwise requested in an enterprise configuration.
- Subprocessor onboarding: we publish a current list and provide 15 business days' notice before onboarding a new subprocessor with an opt-out route (customer can request that data routing be restricted) — we can be flexible for enterprise contracts.

I've attached our proposed redline and the current subprocessor list. Ben will follow up on telemetry retention examples. Let us know the privacy team's must-haves.

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference
(aisha.rahman@redwood.com)

Attachments: CPRA_Addendum_Draft_v1.docx (redline), Subprocessor_List_202611.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Claire Morris <claire.morris@big-retail.com>; Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 09:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: CPRA addendum — telemetry + subprocessors

Claire — quick technical notes on telemetry and re-identification risk:

- Telemetry types: we capture request metadata (timestamps, model id, token counts), server-side resource metrics (GPU/CPU util, queue depth), and anonymized latency/error traces. We do not store customer prompt/content as telemetry.
- Re-identification: telemetry is aggregated and sampled by default. We do not attempt to correlate telemetry with end-user identities; that practice would be inconsistent with our service-provider commitments.
- Retention: 90 days for aggregated metrics; 365 days for security/audit logs. For regulated pilots we can offer custom retention (e.g., 30 days) and scope isolation under Redwood Private deployments.
- Subprocessor notice: our standard is 15 business days. For cases where a subprocessor change is critical to the customer's data flow we can provide earlier notice, and we accept a formal objection process during the notice window.

If helpful I can draft the exact redline language referencing CPRA \"service provider\" obligations and an objection process.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Sr. Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference



From: Claire Morris <claire.morris@big-retail.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Team <legal@big-retail.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: CPRA addendum — telemetry + subprocessors

Thanks Aisha/Ben — this is helpful. Two follow-ups from privacy:

1) Our counsel is pushing back on the 15 business day notice; they'd prefer 10. Is Redwood able to commit to 10 in a narrow set of cases (pilot + low-risk subprocessors)? If not, what alternative mitigations can you offer (e.g., restriction on data routing until customer signs off, or allowing us to require certain subprocessors to be removed from our tenant)?

2) Can you confirm whether telemetry could contain any hashes or identifiers derived from customer content (e.g., prompt fingerprints)? They flagged a scenario where hashes could be used together with other data to re-identify.

We can accept 15 days as a fallback but need a vendor response for our procurement timeline.

Claire



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Claire Morris <claire.morris@big-retail.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 16:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: CPRA addendum — telemetry + subprocessors

Claire —

Appreciate the specificity. Quick answers then a proposed path forward:

- Notice window: we can accept a 10-business-day notice for subprocessors that are considered low-risk (CDNs, observability providers that operate on redacted telemetry) and for pilots where there is explicit scope limitation. For subprocessors that will process content or PII, we need 15 business days and the ability to map data flows as part of onboarding.

- Telemetry fingerprints/hashes: we do not persist customer-derived content hashes as part of standard telemetry. If a customer elects to enable additional debug traces for troubleshooting, those are captured only with customer opt-in and labeled as diagnostics; they are stored under stricter retention and access controls. We'll add explicit language to the addendum confirming no storage of customer-derived identifiers in telemetry by default.

Proposed next steps:
1) I'll circulate a narrow redline to clause 3 (Service Provider obligations) and clause 7 (Subprocessor onboarding) that codifies: 10-business-day notice for low-risk subprocessors; 15 for others; an objection process; and an explicit telemetry non-reidentification statement.
2) Aisha/Ben — can you confirm acceptance so we can get procurement sign-off on Big Retail's timeline?

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Director, Privacy & Compliance Programs — Redwood Inference

Attachment: CPRA_Addendum_Draft_v1.docx (redline v2)

1) Confirmation of which SCCs Redwood plans to flow (we've got a hard internal deadline to confirm EU transfers by end of month).

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Matt O'Neil <matt.oneil@nimbuscare.com>
Date: 2026-11-18T14:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: NimbusCare — Order Form & DPA redlines

Thanks Alex / Matt — I've reviewed the attached order form and DPA redlines. Quick notes (high priority items):

- Roles: we can accept processor-only language for inference requests. Suggest replacing the current generic clause with this sentence: \"Redwood Inference shall act solely as Processor with respect to Customer data processed under the Order Form, consistent with the DPA.\" That keeps the model lifecycle language but clarifies legal position.

- KMS: Our standard for Hosted is Redwood-managed KMS with customer-managed key option (bring-your-own-key) for Dedicated and Private deployments. Proposed language to add to the DPA (short): \"Customer may elect BYOK for Dedicated or Private deployments; for Hosted deployments Redwood will encrypt data at rest using Redwood-managed keys unless Customer requests BYOK and executes the associated order-form option.\"

- SCCs / transfers: the redline references EU SCCs (2021 set). We can support EU SCCs for controller-controller transfers where applicable and will provide the SCC Annex for processor scenarios. We should clarify which entity signatory they want on the SCC (Redwood, Redwood US Sub, Redwood EMEA).

- Transfer-impact addendum: there are two technical asks in the DPA notes (training/retention). I'll put language in the attached DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx to indicate permitted retention windows and a separate checkbox for \"training opt-in\" vs \"training opt-out\" to satisfy NimbusCare's compliance team.

I'll attach a short redline summary (in the tracker) and propose we tidy the order form to include the BYOK option as a selectable line item so procurement can price it.

Attachments: DPA_Technical_Evidence_Matrix.xlsx (xlsx, 85KB)

Ben Carter
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Matt O'Neil <matt.oneil@nimbuscare.com>
Date: 2026-11-19T09:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: NimbusCare — Order Form & DPA redlines

Couple of operational clarifications from privacy/engineering: 

- Dataflow: For Hosted customers we do not route raw production traffic to model training pipelines by default. Any retention for training is opt-in and will be surfaced in the order form as an add-on. I'll mark the exact retention windows we can guarantee (30 / 90 / 180 days) in the evidence matrix.

- SCCs: From a practical standpoint we can support EU SCCs + processor annex for EU transfers. If NimbusCare prefers UK adequacy plus a UK addendum, we need to confirm which legal entity (Redwood UK Ltd) will sign. That affects the contact points on the SCC Annex — please confirm preferred signatory.

- Evidence: For SOC2 / audit evidence we can provide: SOC2 Type II report excerpt, a high-level KMS architecture diagram, and a subprocessor inventory snapshot (last 30 days). We will not share sensitive internal keys or secrets; evidence will be delivered through an NDA-governed portal or secure share.

I recommend we present a compact \"Transfer Impact Addendum\" with: 1) selected SCC/UK addendum; 2) retention checkbox; 3) BYOK selection; 4) listed deliverables with ETA (SOC2 excerpt — 5 business days; subprocessor list — immediate).

Laura Bennett
Privacy Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>, procurement@orionai.com
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@finops.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

Team / OrionAI — kicking off a short weekly thread to align sales / finance / infra on the upcoming OrionAI pilot and potential Dedicated commitment. Summary and asks below.

TL;DR:
- OrionAI is projecting a 12–16 week pilot starting mid-Dec; potential reserve ask of 10-20 reserved GPUs (A100 equiv) if pilot converts.
- Need a fleet plan this week: capacity runway, burst cushion, and cost/latency tradeoffs for a 10/15/20 slot commitment.
- Action: please review attached MA plan (Orion_MA_plan_v2.xlsx) and send initial risk / blockers by Tue 11/17.

Key details from Orion (Ana): expected steady state 1400k tokens/day, peak 3x on training/ingest days; multi-region read from us-west + us-east. They asked about quantized model variants and burst credits.

Questions for infra/finops:
- Ben / Marissa: can we run a quick 3-scenario cost / latency table (10/15/20 reserves) and outline the burst escrow policy?
- Laura: if we need to prioritize latency SLO for Orion, what does that mean for slot allocation vs. other reserved deals next quarter?
- Stephanie: any gating on procurement / legal that will push signature beyond Dec 1? If so, what's our fallback?

Attachments: Orion_MA_plan_v2.xlsx (pilot timeline + MOPs)

Thanks — Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>, Ana Martinez <ana@orionai.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@finops.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly Fleet Playbook — OrionAI pipeline & commit asks

On Fri, Nov 13, 2026 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com> wrote:
> TL;DR: OrionAI ... Need a fleet plan this week...

Quick pass from infra side:
- I ran three modeled scenarios on current pool usage (attached quick sheet) — numbers are rough but directional. See commit-scenarios.pdf.
  - 10 reserved slots: handles ~60% steady requests with bursts handled via shared pool; median latency +35ms vs dedicated.
  - 15 reserved slots: ~85% steady; peak days need ~40% burst from shared pool.
  - 20 reserved slots: covers nearly all steady + 80% of peak, small burst buffer needed.
- Cost delta (monthly): 10->15 ~ +28%; 15->20 ~ +18% (per our price curves).
- Operational note: we can enable KV prefix caching for their chat workloads to reduce token compute by ~12% on steady-state — will need model tokenization profiles from Orion to estimate precisely.

Attached: commit-scenarios.pdf (table + simple cost/latency charts). I can refine numbers if we get their token histogram.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Capacity Planning, Redwood Inference

Quick pass from infra side:
- I ran three modeled scenarios on current pool usage (attached quick sheet) — numbers are rough but directional. See commit-scenarios.pdf.
  - 10 reserved slots: handles ~60% steady requests with bursts handled via shared pool; median latency +35ms vs dedicated.
  - 15 reserved slots: ~85% steady; peak days need ~40% burst from shared pool.
  - 20 reserved slots: covers nearly all steady + 80% of peak, small burst buffer needed.
- Cost delta (monthly): 10->15 ~ +28%; 15->20 ~ +18% (per our price curves).
- Operational note: we can enable KV prefix caching for their chat workloads to reduce token compute by ~12% on steady-state — will need model tokenization profiles from Orion to estimate precisely.

Attached: commit-scenarios.pdf (table + simple cost/latency charts). I can refine numbers if we get their token histogram.

title: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack
preview:
From: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Soto <elena@lumena.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and the concrete next steps we discussed so your team can share with stakeholders. I pasted the Redwood-verified performance snapshots and links below.

TL;DR (recommendation)
- Pilot: 2-week sandbox to validate two model families: rw-instruct-13b-q4f16 (cost-balanced) and rw-70b-8k-q4 (quality option)
- Target SLOs you mentioned: p99 latency <= 250ms for single-turn API, aggregate throughput ~150–200 TPS for peak windows
- We can support a Dedicated VPC pilot; Ben will scope infra and sandbox access.

Call highlights / constraints
- Primary UX: short chat + contextual retrieval (mostly <1k prompt tokens, often repeated prefixes)
- Data sensitivity: customer PII present in prompts; you prefer VPC/private control plane
- Budget: sizing exercise needed — you flagged wanting committed throughput pricing for budgeting

Recommended models (initial slate)
- rw-instruct-13b-q4f16 (Redwood-verified) — balanced quality/cost, good for retrieval-augmented generation. Typical perf (Redwood-hosted, H100-optimized): 220 TPS aggregate on a 4-GPU pool; latencies: p50 18 ms, p95 88 ms, p99 162 ms.
- rw-70b-8k-q4 (Redwood-verified, quantized) — higher generation quality; perf: 48 TPS; latencies: p50 38 ms, p95 160 ms, p99 310 ms.
- redwood/embed-2-small — vector model for retrieval (throughput >1k EPS on a small cluster).

Notes on profiles above
- Numbers are measured with Redwood's continuous-batching runtime and KV-prefix caching enabled; they reflect typical conversational distributions (mix of 64–512 output tokens).
- For your prefix-heavy flows, caching reduces observed p99 by ~20–35% depending on reuse patterns. We'll provide traces to show this.

Attachments & links
- Attached: pilot-proposal-lumena.pdf (scope + mutual action plan), lumena-prompt-pack-v1.json (sample prompt sets we recommend for regression tests), redwood-verified-profiles.csv (raw perf traces)
- Perf/console links: https://app.redwood.ai/models/rw-instruct-13b/perf-profile and https://app.redwood.ai/models/rw-70b/perf-profile

Proposed next steps
1) Agree pilot scope (2 weeks): baseline prompt set, 10k user-simulated queries, 1 endpoint per model variant.
2) Redwood to stand up sandbox (Dedicated VPC option) — Ben to share sandbox access + cost estimate.
3) Procurement: we can provide a short PO + SOW for the pilot; Neha will follow up on contract questions and SOC2 evidence.
4) Kickoff: proposed window Nov 30–Dec 11 (pilot start).

Would you prefer we schedule a 30-minute pilot kickoff next week to lock scope? If so, I can send a calendar invite with Ben and Neha.

Thanks,
Markus
Markus Klein
Head of GTM Engineering
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: pilot-proposal-lumena.pdf (PDF, 2 pages), lumena-prompt-pack-v1.json (JSON, 1.2 KB), redwood-verified-profiles.csv (CSV, 85 KB)

From: Elena Soto <elena@lumena.ai>
To: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Thanks Markus — this is very helpful. A few clarifying questions from our side before we share with procurement: 

1) Are the TPS/latency numbers you listed for hosted Redwood (multi-tenant) or for a Dedicated pool? We need p99 numbers for Dedicated VPC since that's our compliance requirement.
2) Can you share the perf traces for the prefix-caching example you mentioned? I want to show the delta to our infra folks.
3) For the 70B variant: is the 310 ms p99 measured with streaming enabled? We care about perceived latency for end users.
4) Procurement asked for SOC2 evidence and a short template for our PO — can you point us to the right place?

If you can respond with the traces and a short estimate for committed monthly TPS pricing for the Dedicated option, we'll loop procurement and schedule the kickoff.

Best,
Elena Soto
Director of Product
Lumena AI

On Tue, Nov 17, 2026 at 10:05 AM Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Elena — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and the concrete next steps we discussed so your team can share with stakeholders. I pasted the Redwood-verified performance snapshots and links below.

--


From: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Soto <elena@lumena.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Elena — quick answers and attached traces.

1) Dedicated vs hosted: the numbers I quoted in the first mail are from Redwood-hosted baselines on H100 hardware. For Dedicated VPC we run the same runtime but with isolated pools — expect similar p50/p95 latency and slightly improved p99 due to reduced noisy neighbors. I'll attach a short Dedicated snapshot for the 13B variant.

2) Perf traces: attached redwood-verified-profiles.csv (sampled traces) and a small latency trace for the prefix reuse case: https://s3.redwood.ai/public/lumena/prefix-trace-20261117.csv
   - In our prefix reuse test (70% prefix reuse), KV caching reduced p99 by ~28% and median by ~12% compared to no-cache.

3) 70B measurements: the 310 ms p99 I quoted is for non-streaming (full-response latency). With streaming enabled and aggressive chunk sizes you'll see perceived first-byte latencies drop to ~120–160 ms for many prompts; end-to-end completion will still depend on token count.

4) SOC2 / procurement: SOC2 Type II report and standard pilot SOW template are here: https://drive.redwood.ai/security/soc2-type2-2026.pdf and https://drive.redwood.ai/templates/pilot-sow-lumena.docx

Action items I'm taking:
- Ben: please provision a Dedicated sandbox (VPC) and share credentials + a short cost estimate for a 4-GPU pool sized to ~150 TPS baseline.
- Neha: I'll let you follow up on contract/PO language and any compliance questions.
- Elena: we can lock pilot dates Nov 30–Dec 11 as proposed; Ben will propose exact slots once sandbox is up.

Ben (cc) — can you confirm sandbox ETA and a rough monthly committed-TPS price for a modest 150 TPS committed tier?

Thanks,
Markus

--
Attachments: redwood-verified-profiles.csv (CSV, 85 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Soto <elena@lumena.ai>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: model fit recommendations & perf pack

Hi Elena — quick update from infra/procurement side: 

- Sandbox/VPC: I can provision a Dedicated sandbox (4x H100, single-tenant) and will have credentials to you by Mon Nov 22 EOD. This will mirror the environment used for the Dedicated perf snapshots.
- Cost: approximate committed tier pricing for 150 TPS sustained on the 13B profile is attached as a short estimate in the portal; rough ballpark: $32k–$45k/month depending on commit length and burst allowances. I will formalize a pilot PO template once procurement confirms billing entity.
- Procurement docs: I've linked the SOC2 and our standard pilot SOW in the shared Drive. If procurement wants, we can start a short reseller/PO workflow — Elena, who should I expect on your procurement side?

Next steps
1) Elena to introduce procurement contact (procurement@lumena.ai) or preferred PO recipient.
2) I’ll send sandbox invites and a short cost estimate sheet by Monday; then we can lock pilot window Nov 30–Dec 11.

Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: Urgent: damaged embroidered jackets in transit — remediation and local reprint plan
preview:
From: Maria Gonzalez <maria@sewnrightpromo.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: BrightStream jackets shipment — transit damage (photos attached)

title: Align on evaluation kickoff + technical deep dive availability and success metrics
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@medicahealth.com>, Tom Evans <tom.evans@medicahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 16:22:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery recap + proposed slots for eval kickoff and tech deep dive

Hi Claire / Tom —

Thanks again for the call today — great to hear how MedicaHealth is thinking about using large language models to accelerate coding of discharge summaries and to triage inbound clinical messages. Quick recap from our side and proposed next steps so we can lock the evaluation kickoff and the technical deep dive: 

Recap (high level)
- Goal: reduce manual discharge coding time by 40% and maintain >95% accuracy on coded fields for first-pass outputs.
- Data: de-identified historical notes (~120k docs) for prompt tuning; integrations: S3 export -> Redwood Private during evaluation.
- Success signals: prompt-level accuracy on held-out set, latency within 200ms/token for 99th pct, and end-to-end throughput to support 5 concurrent users.

Proposed next meetings
1) Eval kickoff (90m) — review mutual action plan, trial configuration, prompt set and evaluation criteria.
2) Technical deep dive (60m) — engineering run-through: access, SSO, ingestion pipeline, and security questions (data retention, audit logs).

Proposed slots (PST) — please pick one for the kickoff and we’ll schedule the deep dive the following day: 
- Tue Nov 23, 10:00–11:30 AM PST
- Thu Nov 25, 1:00–2:30 PM PST

Prep we’ll bring to the kickoff
- Suggested prompt pack + initial evaluation harness (we'll share before the call)
- Trial plan draft with timeline and owners (HubSpot link below)
- Security playbook for Private deployment (SOC2 items highlighted) — can review during tech deep dive.

Attachments: discovery-recap.pdf (notes), proposed-kickoff-slots.ics
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/contact/987654321
Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1abcDEF_kickoff-plan

If the slots above don’t work, send 2 alternatives and we'll accommodate. Once you confirm the kickoff slot I’ll send calendar invites and a short pre-reading pack (prompt examples + evaluation checklist).

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0117



From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@medicahealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Evans <tom.evans@medicahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-discovery recap + proposed slots for eval kickoff and tech deep dive

Hi Aisha —

Thanks for the clear recap and proposed slots. Thu Nov 25 at 1:00 PM PST works best for our team for the kickoff. We can do the technical deep dive the following day if that’s still available.

A few quick notes/questions to prep the kickoff:
- Security/compliance: can you confirm whether Redwood Private supports short-term audit log export and whether we can validate retention settings for the trial environment? We expect our security reviewer to ask for SOC2 mapping during the tech deep dive.
- Latency SLO: our clinical app needs 95th pct latency under 250ms; is that a threshold you can target during the trial?
- Prompt examples: we will share 10 sample prompts and a small holdout set ahead of the kickoff (I'll send later today).

Attaching Tom — he’ll join the tech deep dive from infra and will share our Okta admin contact for SSO configuration.

Thanks — looking forward to locking this in.
Claire Huang
Director, Clinical Apps | MedicaHealth
claire.huang@medicahealth.com

1) Okta / SSO: can you share the Okta admin contact (or the SAML metadata) ahead of the tech deep dive? Karthik will need that to validate the SSO flow for the Private evaluation.

title: Nested remit waterfall for multi-subsidiary invoicing — proposal and next steps
preview:
From: Miriam Shaw <miriam.shaw@helixtx.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@helixtx.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 15:02:00 -0500
Subject: Multi-entity invoicing — remit routing & VAT handling

Hi Karthik,

Hope you’re well. We’re finalizing our vendor onboarding for Q1 and need to confirm how Redwood will handle invoices and remittances across our legal entities. We have three bill-to entities (Helix US, Helix EU, Helix APAC) and a mix of central cost centers. Specific asks:

- Can Redwood issue a single customer-facing invoice that splits line items to the three entities for accounting?
- Will the invoice show VAT per EU entity and include VAT IDs on-line level?
- We need the ability to map seat counts to cost centers so our internal chargebacks can post correctly.
- Is there a recommended flow for remittance (one payment to Redwood with allocations versus separate payments)?

We’re also sensitive to PO/contract signatures and would prefer a sample split-invoice layout we can circulate with Accounting and Procurement. Could you point us to a template or a short proposal?

Thanks,
Miriam
Miriam Shaw
Head of Procurement
Helix Therapeutics

--
Attachment: none


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Miriam Shaw <miriam.shaw@helixtx.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Multi-entity invoicing — remit routing & VAT handling

Hi Miriam — thanks for the clear list. TL;DR: yes, we can support a single invoice with per-line entity and VAT breakdowns and provide CSV/line-level mappings for your chargeback process. Details and a short proposal below.

Proposed approach:
- Single consolidated invoice PDF for your AP team that contains: per-line legal entity allocation, VAT ID shown for EU lines, and a per-line cost center tag.
- Remittance options: (A) one payment to Redwood with an accompanying allocation file we apply (preferred operationally), or (B) separate payments per legal entity. We recommend (A) with a nested remit waterfall so your treasury makes one payment and we settle internally across our ledgers.
- We will supply a sample split invoice (xlsx) and a brief remit waterfall proposal for review.

Next steps we propose:
1) Share your legal entity names + tax IDs (if you’re comfortable);
2) We’ll send a sample invoice + a short PDF describing the nested remit waterfall and the processing SLA;
3) Procurement/Legal review DocuSign SOW/PO if needed — we can prepare the DocuSign-ready SOW with the split-invoice appendix.

Looping Ben (payments) and Laura (billing ops) so they can weigh in on ledger tagging and timing.

Best,
Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: none


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 11:45:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Multi-entity invoicing — remit routing & VAT handling

Karthik / Laura — drafting an operational plan now. Points to include in the proposal PDF:

- Ledger tagging: we'll include a 'bill_entity' and 'charge_costcenter' field at line level. That will map to our accounts receivable posting and to your incoming AR mapping.
- Nested remit waterfall: sequence = (1) apply centralized payment to primary legal entity (Redwood US), (2) internal settlement to Redwood EU / APAC ledgers using intercompany offsets, (3) VAT reclaim handling where applicable.
- Timing: If you choose single-payment flow (our recommendation), funds posted T+1 to primary ledger; intercompany settlement runs T+2–T+3 depending on currency rails.
- Audit artifacts: we’ll include an allocation CSV and a PDF invoice with footnote traceability.

I attached a short proposal doc (nested_remit_proposal.pdf) and a sample invoice layout. Want to make sure we call out VAT reverse-charge options for Helix EU if they qualify.

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Payments & Receivables
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: nested_remit_proposal.pdf (1 page), sample_split_invoice.xlsx (template)


From: Miriam Shaw <miriam.shaw@helixtx.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@helixtx.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Nov 2026 08:30:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Multi-entity invoicing — remit routing & VAT handling

title: PaperTrail expansion & renewal — post-call recap and next steps
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Zhang <olivia@papertrail.ai>, Rajiv Mehta <rajiv@papertrail.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: PaperTrail expansion & renewal — post-call recap and next steps

Thanks again for taking the time earlier today. Quick recap of what we discussed and proposed next steps so everyone has a single source of truth. Please call out any corrections.

What we heard (pain + goals)
- Peak latency spikes during nightly ingestion windows (95th percentile hits ~1.2s) impacting index refresh and downstream search quality.
- Overall token spend has grown 3.5x year over year; team is looking to contain unit costs without sacrificing response quality.
- New workloads: (1) higher-concurrency RAG for customer support (short context, low-latency), (2) nightly batch embeddings job for updated knowledge base, and (3) a small real-time summarization endpoint for internal QA.
- Security/compliance: PaperTrail will need SOC 2 evidence + logs retention options for audit next quarter.

Redwood's recommended starting point
1) Short-term: run a 30-day Dedicated pilot (reserved capacity pool) sized for current 95th percentile concurrency with burst to handle nightly jobs. This reduces per-token costs and stabilizes latency.
2) Tech optimizations during pilot: KV/prefix caching for RAG, mixed-precision quantization on the embeddings model, and request-level batching for the nightly job.
3) Parallel path: have Legal/Procurement start procurement checklist while SEs run technical sizing and a Deep Technical Review (DTR).

Action items & owners (proposed)
- Aisha: Deliver the usage export and initial sizing recommendation (attached) — today.
- Grace (SE): Run a technical deep-dive and pilot plan (45–60 min) — week of Nov 16.
- Ben (AE): Prepare commercial outline for a 6‑month Dedicated reservation, with optional burst capacity and a preferred renewal discount — by Nov 18.
- Olivia / Rajiv: Share the list of internal stakeholders for security review and confirm target procurement timeline (expected Nov 30 internal approval).

Attachments shared in this email:
- papertrail_usage_nov2026.csv (usage export)
- rtm_sizing_recommendation.pdf (initial sizing + expected latency/cost impact)
- mutual_action_plan_PaperTrail.xlsx (M.A.P. draft)

Next meetings
- Tech deep-dive w/ Grace: proposing Tue, Nov 17 10:00–11:00 PT (I can send a calendar invite once you confirm).
- Commercial sync (ben + procurement): Thu, Nov 19 09:00–09:30 PT.

If that aligns, I will: (a) lock the two meeting times, (b) share a HubSpot deal link and the MAP in Drive, and (c) start routing SOC2 pieces to your security contact.

Thanks — looking forward to moving this forward.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
https://redwoodinference.com

--
Attachments: papertrail_usage_nov2026.csv (21 KB), rtm_sizing_recommendation.pdf (320 KB), mutual_action_plan_PaperTrail.xlsx (48 KB)

Cc: Rajiv Mehta <rajiv@papertrail.ai>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 09:24:00 -0800
Subject: Re: PaperTrail expansion & renewal — post-call recap and next steps

Thanks Aisha — this is a great summary and mostly matches our notes. A couple quick clarifications and asks: 

- Concurrency: nightly ingestion concurrency was overstated on our side earlier. 95th percentile concurrent requests for the nightly job is closer to 220 (not 400) — this lowers the short-term Dedicated sizing you outlined.
- Security: yes, SOC 2 evidence is required. Additionally, our procurement team will want to confirm data residency controls (logs retained for 12 months) and any support SLAs around incident response for production inference. I'll loop Legal/Procurement once we have the commercial outline.
- New workload priority: RAG (customer support) > embeddings (nightly) > summarization. We want the pilot to demonstrate consistent sub-200ms median latency for the RAG path under realistic load.

I attached our internal procurement checklist (procurement_checklist_PT.pdf) — it's not exhaustive but should help Ben with commercial framing. Rajiv and I are available for the tech deep-dive on Tue Nov 17; either 10:00 or 11:30 PT works.

On the pilot timeline, is a 30-day pilot still the recommendation, or would Redwood prefer an initial 14-day proof-of-concept followed by a 30-day ramp? We want to minimize interruption to our production ingestion.

Appreciate the quick turnaround here.

Best,
Olivia Zhang
Head of Platform Engineering | PaperTrail AI

---

title: Request: soft-preemption carveout & tail-latency guarantees for reserved nodes
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Patel <samira@helixai.com>, Todd Lee <todd.ops@aurora.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: soft-preemption carveout & tail-latency guarantees for reserved nodes

Hi Samira / Todd —

Thanks again for the deep dive yesterday. Per HelixAI's high-frequency inference workloads, you asked us to document a proposal to reduce observed end-to-end 95-99.9th tail latency variance when using Dedicated reserved GPUs, specifically around preemption behavior and nightly batch windows.

We are proposing a \"soft-preemption carveout\" for up to 10% of a customer's reserved node pool during their critical windows (your peak inference hours). Under the carveout:
- Reserved nodes in the carveout will not be eligible for preemptible bursting or eviction for a configurable window (default 4 hours/day).
- Queueing policy will prioritize pinned requests from this carveout with a capped concurrency multiplier to avoid starvation.
- If capacity pressure forces temporary routing, requests will fail-over to a compatible quantized model variant with degraded SLO but automatic alerting to Helix ops.

We think this approach balances platform flexibility and your requirement for predictable tail behavior. Attached is a draft SLA clause (helixai_sla_draft_v3.pdf) and a small sample of traces illustrating the tail events in question (tail_latency_sample_traces.zip).

Can you confirm the typical daily window you want pinned? Also want to check whether Helix prefers hard guarantees (SLA credit formula) or a contractual playbook (runbook + credits on major violations).

Next steps I propose: internal engineering alignment at Redwood (Ben + David) and a short runbook draft for the carveout. If Helix signs off, we’ll schedule a 2-week pilot on a small slice of your reserved pool.

Thanks,
Aisha
---
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachments: helixai_sla_draft_v3.pdf (PDF), tail_latency_sample_traces.zip (ZIP)

From: Samira Patel <samira@helixai.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Todd Lee <todd.ops@aurora.ai>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: soft-preemption carveout & tail-latency guarantees for reserved nodes

Aisha —

Thanks for the clear write-up and the draft SLA. Helix is definitely aligned on the need for a carveout window. For our production traffic the busiest window is US-PST 14:00-18:00 (we run spike-prone inference during east-coast business hours). A few clarifications: 

1) We need the carveout to cover at least 25% of our reserved pool during that window (not 10%). Can Redwood support a higher carveout percentage?
2) The fail-over to a quantized model variant is acceptable only if the degradation is <12% in quality (we will provide the eval set).
3) On credits: we'd prefer an SLA credit that has a clear formula (minutes below threshold => credits), but we'll consider a hybrid playbook if Redwood can guarantee a mitigation timeline (e.g., within 30 minutes of an incident, automated fallback and a manual remediation path).

Todd and I are available for a pilot kickoff next week. Please let us know what you need from our side (telemetry, eval prompts, or account access).

Best,
Samira
---
Samira Patel
Head of Platform Reliability, HelixAI
samira@helixai.com


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:27:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: soft-preemption carveout & tail-latency guarantees for reserved nodes

Aisha / Lauren —

Read Samira's ask. Operational notes from infra:
- We can support a 25% carveout, but it changes engineering constraints: carveout nodes will be excluded from multi-tenant preemption and burst pools. That means we need a dedicated headroom budget; effectively ~1.15x token-hour commitment for capacity planning (to account for warmups/heterogeneous load).
- Queueing change: we'll implement a weighted priority queue for pinned traffic with a concurrency cap. Key risk: if pinned traffic spikes beyond provisioned concurrency, requests will queue and potentially hit client-side timeouts. We should set a recommended client retry policy and a server-side circuit breaker (configurable).
- For the quality degradation requirement on quantized fallback, we need Helix evals to validate. If the quantized variant fails the <12% threshold, we will negotiate a different fallback (e.g., offload to a small burst of on-demand instances for short periods).

1) Aisha: ask Helix to provide the eval set + expected acceptable throughput during the window.
2) Lauren: draft a simple runbook + suggested SLA credit formula (minutes below 95th/99th thresholds => credit percent).
3) David: confirm whether our monitoring can detect a soft-preemption event and auto-trigger fallback within your 30 min target. If not, propose automation work estimate.

Ben

Subject: Request: soft-preemption carveout & tail-latency guarantees for reserved nodes

Hi Samira / Todd —

Thanks again for the deep dive yesterday. Per HelixAI's high-frequency inference workloads, you asked us to document a proposal to reduce observed end-to-end 95-99.9th tail latency variance when using Dedicated reserved GPUs, specifically around preemption behavior and nightly batch windows.

We are proposing a \"soft-preemption carveout\" for up to 10% of a customer's reserved node pool during their critical windows (your peak inference hours). Under the carveout:
- Reserved nodes in the carveout will not be eligible for preemptible bursting or eviction for a configurable window (default 4 hours/day).
- Queueing policy will prioritize pinned requests from this carveout with a capped concurrency multiplier to avoid starvation.
- If capacity pressure forces temporary routing, requests will fail-over to a compatible quantized model variant with degraded SLO but automatic alerting to Helix ops.

We think this approach balances platform flexibility and your requirement for predictable tail behavior. Attached is a draft SLA clause (helixai_sla_draft_v3.pdf) and a small sample of traces illustrating the tail events in question (tail_latency_sample_traces.zip).

Can you confirm the typical daily window you want pinned? Also want to check whether Helix prefers hard guarantees (SLA credit formula) or a contractual playbook (runbook + credits on major violations).

Next steps I propose: internal engineering alignment at Redwood (Ben + David) and a short runbook draft for the carveout. If Helix signs off, we’ll schedule a 2-week pilot on a small slice of your reserved pool.

Thanks,
Aisha

1) We need the carveout to cover at least 25% of our reserved pool during that window (not 10%). Can Redwood support a higher carveout percentage?
2) The fail-over to a quantized model variant is acceptable only if the degradation is <12% in quality (we will provide the eval set).
3) On credits: we'd prefer an SLA credit that has a clear formula (minutes below threshold => credits), but we'll consider a hybrid playbook if Redwood can guarantee a mitigation timeline (e.g., within 30 minutes of an incident, automated fallback and a manual remediation path).

Todd and I are available for a pilot kickoff next week. Please let us know what you need from our side (telemetry, eval prompts, or account access).

title: Operationalizing streaming funcalls + ranking — recap & next steps
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sam Levin <sam.levin@monarchhealth.com>, Priya Rao <priya.rao@monarchhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 15:05:00 -0800
Subject: Operationalizing streaming funcalls + ranking — recap & next steps

Hi Sam / Priya — thanks again for taking the time today. Short recap from our call and proposed next steps so we’re all aligned.

Key pain you described
- Clinical triage team needs sub-second follow-up suggestions based on multi-turn intake (streaming) but current endpoint stalls on long context.
- Existing pipeline has a reranking stage (BM25 -> model rerank) that’s adding 200–400ms per request and is brittle for new document types (discharge notes).
- Security needs: logs of requests retained 90 days, and model outputs must be redactable/traceable for audits.

Goals we agreed on
- Prototype a streaming + funcall flow that produces partial responses while calling into a structured-output function to populate triage fields.
- Replace the brittle rerank step with an embeddings + light model reranker (embedding similarity candidate recall + small reranker model) to reduce latency and improve stability.
- Deliver a short pilot activity: 2-week sandbox, integration checklist, and a low-risk canary to validate latency & correctness.

Technical constraints you mentioned
- Max end-to-end P95 latency target: 350ms for simple triage flows.
- Maximum 2k token working set in the client (we will suggest KV caching for static patient snippets).
- SOC2 + SCIM provisioning required before sandbox access.

Immediate next steps (owners & timing)
1) Redwood: share a short integration checklist + sample prompt set (attachment) — by Thu 11/12 — owner: Ava.
2) Monarch: provide a representative triage conversation log (anonymized) + a list of must-have structured fields — by Mon 11/16 — owner: Priya.
3) Tech sync (30m) to walk the SDK changes required for streaming and funcalls: Thu 11/19 (propose 10am PT) — owners: Ben + Sam.
4) Security kickoff for SOC2 evidence & SCIM: week of 11/23 — owner: Sergio.

Follow-ups we’ll include in the proposal
- Option A: streaming + funcall path using Redwood streaming SDK + structured output schema. Estimated infra delta: low (we’ll reuse your pool of small vector store nodes).
- Option B: batching + cached KV approach for longer contexts if streaming cannot meet 350ms in initial pilot.

Attachments: integration-proposal.pdf (proposal outline), sample-prompt-set.csv (example prompts + reranker seed queries).

If that reads correctly, I’ll send a formal sandbox invite and share the Drive folder with the proposal.

Thanks — Ava

--
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192
https://redwood.inference |  


From: Sam Levin <sam.levin@monarchhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya.rao@monarchhealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 17:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Operationalizing streaming funcalls + ranking — recap & next steps

Thanks Ava — that summary is spot on. A couple clarifications from our side: 

- The 350ms P95 is for the 3–5 field triage flow (the longer flows can be 600–700ms while we still feel comfortable).
- The reranker is currently a heuristic layer that drops many relevant docs; replacing with lightweight reranker + embedding candidates sounds promising.
- We can send an anonymized week of triage logs (approx 1k sessions) but need to scrub PHI — Priya will own.

Agree on the proposed next steps. Confirming Thu 11/19 10am PT works for Ben + your team. Please include a short demo of the streaming SDK behavior (partial tokens + structured funcall JSON) in the sync.

Sam
Director, Clinical Systems
Monarch Health

>

title: Zenotech - audit log retention partitioning & sample export plan
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>, Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the RFP submission earlier this week. A couple of follow-ups from our compliance team: we need clarity on how Redwood partitions audit logs by tenant and activity class (admin events vs. content events), how retention windows are enforced per partition, and how exports (full vs. delta) are delivered.

Specifically: 1) Can you provide a sample export (<=10k lines) showing manifest format, per-record metadata (tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, timestamp), and tamper-evidence hashes? 2) Can retention be tiered per partition (30d/180d/3yr) and enforced automatically with verifiable deletion timestamps? 3) What SLA and timeline do you recommend for bulk export (30TB) vs. scheduled incremental exports?

If possible, please attach a sample manifest and a technical note on partitioning. We are on a tight timeline for our audit next month.

Regards,
Liam
GRC Lead, Zenotech Financial



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
Cc: Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>, Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Liam —

Thanks — we can do all three items. I attached a short export manifest (CSV) and a technical note that outlines our partitioning model and retention enforcement. High level summary below:

- Partitioning: logs are sharded by tenant_id and event_class. Each partition has its own immutable append-only ledger and manifest.
- Retention policy: per-partition retention policies are configured via the Console or API (e.g., 30d, 180d, 3yr). Deletions are performed via immutable tombstones and recorded in a deletion ledger that captures deletion_time, operator_id, and a verifiable hash of the pre-deletion manifest.
- Export types: full export (snapshot) and delta export (since-seq-id). Exports include a manifest with per-record metadata and SHA-256 item hashes for tamper evidence.
- SLAs/timelines: for a 30TB bulk export we typically quote 7–10 business days to prepare, validate, and stage an encrypted S3 transfer; incremental scheduled exports (hourly/daily) are available with nearline delivery (minutes to hours) depending on requested chunk size and routing.

I'll loop in engineering for the sample and a clear timeline. In the meantime, attaching: redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf and sample-export-manifest.csv.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Zenotech - retention partitioning & sample export plan (coordination)

Ben/Marissa — forwarding Zenotech's follow-up. They want a <=10k-line sample manifest and a technical note on per-partition deletion evidence plus an estimated timeline for a 30TB bulk export.

Ask: can engineering produce a geared sample that includes tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, timestamp, item_hash, and an attached CSV they can consume? Also need a short paragraph describing how deletions are recorded (deletion ledger + tombstone hash).

Goal: send the sample + a concise SLA/timeline back by end of day Monday 11/9 so they have it for their audit prep.

Thanks —
Aisha



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T13:40:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Zenotech - retention partitioning & sample export plan (coordination)

Aisha —

We can produce the sample manifest and a short technical blurb. Notes from infra:
- We'll generate a synthetic 10k-line CSV with the exact schema Zenotech requested. Each line will include tenant_id, event_class (admin|auth|content|system), sequence_id, iso_timestamp, payload_length, sha256(item), and a partition_digest for the batch.
- Deletion flow: when retention policy expires we atomically write a tombstone record to the deletion ledger (tombstone_id, deletion_time, policy_id, operator_id) and include the pre-delete partition_manifest_hash. These logs are immutable and exported as part of compliance bundles.
- Bulk export cadence and sizing: for 30TB we propose a three-stage plan — prepare (validate manifests, encryption keys) 2-3 days, stage & transfer (multi-part encrypted S3 upload) 3-6 days depending on egress bandwidth, final verification 1 day. Recommend 7 business days baseline; accelerate to 4 with priority engineering and additional egress capacity (cost adder).

I'll drop the synthetic CSV and a chain-of-custody template for their legal team.

Ben
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
Cc: Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>, Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-10T14:02:00-08:00
Subject: RE: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Liam / team —

Attached are the items requested for your compliance review:

1) sample-export-manifest.csv — 9,872 synthetic lines (schema described below).
2) redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf — short note on partitioning and deletion ledger semantics.
3) chain_of_custody_template.docx — template we use for evidence handoffs.

Quick schema summary (also included in the CSV header): tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, iso_timestamp, payload_type, payload_length, sha256_item_hash, partition_batch_id, partition_digest, export_manifest_id. The manifest includes a top-level partition_manifest_hash and a signing signature using our HSM-backed key.

Retention & deletion evidence (summary):
- Per-partition retention is configurable by policy and enforced by automated tombstone issuance.
- Tombstone records are appended to a deletion ledger which itself is exported with every archive; each ledger file includes pre-delete manifest_hash and a cryptographic digest chain to detect tampering.
- We can provide an audited manifest and sample deletion ledger excerpt for any given tenant range upon request.

Export timelines:
- Delta (since-seq) exports: typically minutes–hours depending on window and size; ideal for nearline ingestion.
- Bulk 30TB export: baseline 7 business days (prepare + stage + transfer + verify). Priority path: 4 business days with additional network egress and an expedited engineering run. Cost/ops estimate included in the PDF.

Next steps: please confirm whether Zenotech would like a prioritized 4-day run (we will need written confirmation and a procurement PO to allocate dedicated egress). If not, we will plan for the 7-business-day standard run.

Thanks and happy to jump on a quick call if you prefer to review the manifests together.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf, sample-export-manifest.csv, chain_of_custody_template.docx
