title: Service advisory: elevated 503 pattern impacting hosted generation endpoints
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:12:00 -0800
Subject: Notice: elevated 503 pattern on hosted generation endpoints (investigating)

Hi Priyanka,

I wanted to let you know we’re currently seeing an elevated pattern of 503 responses on hosted generation endpoints (primarily POST /v1/generate). Your traffic from Clarion.ai appears to be affected in the US region this morning — we’re prioritizing impact for live production calls and we’ve opened an incident to track this.

What we know so far:
- Start time (observed): 2026-11-10 08:05 PST
- Symptom: intermittent 503 responses, bursty errors, increased p95 latency for generation requests
- Scope: subset of hosted capacity in us-west; SDK and REST clients both impacted

What we’re doing:
- Engineering is on an incident bridge now and looking at autoscaling/controller events and recent deploys.
- We’ve added a temporary routing rule to reduce load on affected pools for high-priority customers.

Immediate ask: are you seeing a specific API endpoint or route that’s most critical for you? If so, reply with an example request ID and we’ll target that flow.

We’ll post live updates to the incident page: https://status.redwood.com/incidents/RED-20261110-1 and the bridge link below.

Thanks for the patience — I know this hurts traffic for you. I’ll follow up within 30 minutes with an update or earlier if we have a mitigation.

Bridge: https://join.redwood.com/bridge/RED-20261110-1
Attachment: initial-error-graph.png (PNG, 74 KB) — the first minute of error-rate by backend pool.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference



From: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:18:30 -0800
Subject: Re: Notice: elevated 503 pattern on hosted generation endpoints (investigating)

Alex —

Thanks for the heads up. We’re seeing failures in our live inference path (user-facing generation) and it’s causing request spikes and retries. Example failing request id: req_8f3a2b5d (timestamp 08:16 PST).

Can you confirm whether Clarion.ai customers are being routed to any fallback model or older instance groups? Also, we’ll need an ETA — this is affecting a portion of our paying users and product is tracking errors to the team.

If you can provide a short update to our SRE and PM (copied) it’d help us manage the incident internally.

—Priyanka
Priyanka Rao
Head of Platform @ Clarion.ai
priya@clarion.ai



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:25:05 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Re: Notice: elevated 503 pattern on hosted generation endpoints (investigating)

Team — forwarding Priyanka’s note. Quick thoughts:
- We should proactively offer a temporary credit for impacted production calls if this looks multi-hour.
- Ops/Eng ask: can we throttle non-customer background traffic in affected pools to preserve live traffic? If yes, coordinate on the bridge.
- Comms: Alex, please include language around ETA and whether Dedicated/fallback routing is available. PR should be factual and concise.

I’ll be ready to help run customer comms once we have confirmed mitigation steps.

-Ben
Head of Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Clarion Support <support@clarion.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:02:40 -0800
Subject: Status update: mitigation in progress (RED-20261110-1)

Priyanka — quick update from the bridge.

Summary of current status:
- Root cause (preliminary): an autoscaler controller regression in our routing plane that incorrectly trimmed warm backends during a scaling event; this produced short-lived capacity gaps and a cascade of 503s for affected pools.
- Scope: hosted generation pools in us-west only; we are not seeing similar patterns in eu or apac at this time.
- Impact: intermittent 503s and elevated p95 for generation calls between 08:05–08:55 PST; error rate reduced substantially after a mitigation at ~08:58 PST.

Mitigations applied:
- Reverted the autoscaler config change and forced a slow rewarm of removed instances.
- Temporarily pinned traffic from Clarion.ai to a higher-priority serving pool and bypassed one failing controller.
- Read-only metric collection retained for postmortem; we’ve attached the most relevant logs.

Next ETA:
- We expect to have full stable capacity restored and verification completed by 10:00 PST. We will keep the pinned routing until verification passes a 30‑minute stability window.

Attachments: clarion-logs-20261110.txt (text, 130 KB) — redacted request ids and backend traces.

We understand the customer impact and Ben is coordinating a credits/comp package. We’ll send a short incident summary and a proposed credit offer by end of day.

If you want a synchronous sync, join the bridge: https://join.redwood.com/bridge/RED-20261110-1 (ops only link)

Thanks for the patience — we’ll follow with a post-incident write-up and proposed remediation timeline.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Follow-up: incident RED-20261110-1 — summary and next steps

Priyanka —

Final note from the incident today. Full timeline and summary below for your records.

Timeline (summary):
- 08:05 PST: elevated 503s begin (autoscaler trimming observed)
- 08:12 PST: customer notifications and bridge opened
- 08:25 PST: internal comms on credits/mitigation
- 08:58 PST: mitigation applied (revert + pinned routing)
- 09:02 PST: error rate reduced ~95%
- 09:40 PST: verification checks started; no recurrence seen
- 10:00 PST: capacity restored and monitoring window began

Root cause (final, preliminary): a controller regression pushed in the autoscaler staging pipeline that had insufficient guards for simultaneous drain + scale operations; during a rapid traffic shift, the controller marked warm nodes as removable and a gap in capacity caused 503 spikes. We have reverted the change and are hardening the guardrails.

Customer impact: Clarion.ai experienced increased client-side retries and ~2–5% of production requests returned 503 during the window. We estimate the bulk of errors occurred between 08:05–08:58 PST.

Actions we’re taking (next steps):
- Code fix: roll back the problematic controller change and add unit/integration tests to prevent similar trims (timeline: fix merged and staged, release planned within 48 hours).
- Platform changes: add an additional safe-check in the autoscaler to prevent simultaneous drain+scale during high request churn.
- Postmortem: detailed postmortem doc and RCA will be shared by 2026-11-12 EOD.
- Compensation: Ben will send a proposed credits package within 24 hours.

Attachments: postmortem-outline-draft.pdf (PDF, 85 KB) — preliminary postmortem outline and affected metrics.

We are sorry for the disruption. If you’d like a review call to go through the postmortem and remediation plan, please propose times and we’ll set one up.

Regards,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference

> Priyanka — quick update from the bridge.
> 
> Summary of current status:...

Team — forwarding Priyanka’s note. Quick thoughts:

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Clarion Support <support@clarion.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:02:40 -0800
Subject: Status update: mitigation in progress (RED-20261110-1)

Priyanka — quick update from the bridge.

Summary of current status:
- Root cause (preliminary): an autoscaler controller regression in our routing plane that incorrectly trimmed warm backends during a scaling event; this produced short-lived capacity gaps and a cascade of 503s for affected pools.
- Scope: hosted generation pools in us-west only; we are not seeing similar patterns in eu or apac at this time.
- Impact: intermittent 503s and elevated p95 for generation calls between 08:05–08:55 PST; error rate reduced substantially after a mitigation at ~08:58 PST.

Mitigations applied:
- Reverted the autoscaler config change and forced a slow rewarm of removed instances.
- Temporarily pinned traffic from Clarion.ai to a higher-priority serving pool and bypassed one failing controller.
- Read-only metric collection retained for postmortem; we’ve attached the most relevant logs.

Next ETA:
- We expect to have full stable capacity restored and verification completed by 10:00 PST. We will keep the pinned routing until verification passes a 30‑minute stability window.

Attachments: clarion-logs-20261110.txt (text, 130 KB) — redacted request ids and backend traces.

We understand the customer impact and Ben is coordinating a credits/comp package. We’ll send a short incident summary and a proposed credit offer by end of day.

If you want a synchronous sync, join the bridge: https://join.redwood.com/bridge/RED-20261110-1 (ops only link)

Thanks for the patience — we’ll follow with a post-incident write-up and proposed remediation timeline.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Follow-up: incident RED-20261110-1 — summary and next steps

Priyanka —

Final note from the incident today. Full timeline and summary below for your records.

Timeline (summary):
- 08:05 PST: elevated 503s begin (autoscaler trimming observed)
- 08:12 PST: customer notifications and bridge opened
- 08:25 PST: internal comms on credits/mitigation
- 08:58 PST: mitigation applied (revert + pinned routing)
- 09:02 PST: error rate reduced ~95%
- 09:40 PST: verification checks started; no recurrence seen
- 10:00 PST: capacity restored and monitoring window began

Root cause (final, preliminary): a controller regression pushed in the autoscaler staging pipeline that had insufficient guards for simultaneous drain + scale operations; during a rapid traffic shift, the controller marked warm nodes as removable and a gap in capacity caused 503 spikes. We have reverted the change and are hardening the guardrails.

Customer impact: Clarion.ai experienced increased client-side retries and ~2–5% of production requests returned 503 during the window. We estimate the bulk of errors occurred between 08:05–08:58 PST.

Actions we’re taking (next steps):
- Code fix: roll back the problematic controller change and add unit/integration tests to prevent similar trims (timeline: fix merged and staged, release planned within 48 hours).
- Platform changes: add an additional safe-check in the autoscaler to prevent simultaneous drain+scale during high request churn.
- Postmortem: detailed postmortem doc and RCA will be shared by 2026-11-12 EOD.
- Compensation: Ben will send a proposed credits package within 24 hours.

Attachments: postmortem-outline-draft.pdf (PDF, 85 KB) — preliminary postmortem outline and affected metrics.

We are sorry for the disruption. If you’d like a review call to go through the postmortem and remediation plan, please propose times and we’ll set one up.

Regards,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

Date: 2026-11-07T15:04:00-08:00
Subject: Advance shipment - bonding / IOR question

Hi Ava,

We have the advance pallet shipment (BOL 84231) inbound to the staging warehouse for your SF show. The venue has flagged a bonding requirement and is asking who the Importer of Record (IOR) should be. The paperwork we received lists the venue as consignee but does not state IOR explicitly.

Quick questions: can you confirm Redwood's preference for IOR on this shipment (Redwood Inference / client IOR / venue IOR)? If Redwood will be IOR we need the EIN and authorized signer for release. If the venue must be IOR, please confirm contact and signed authorization.

Also FYI: the warehouse sent a cutoff note saying freight must be admitted by 11/08 09:00 local — they did not specify timezone in the notice. I flagged this as potentially PT but wanted to confirm.

Attaching the BOL and the warehouse notice we were given.

Thanks,
James
James Smith
Pacific Freight Co. — Account Executive

title: Dedicated capacity floorplan & surge architecture - Lumos Bio
preview:
From: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency numbers looked strong for our baseline load. We’re starting to firm up our infrastructure ask for Q2 2027 and want to include Redwood Dedicated in the procurement packet. Before we loop procurement I need a concise set of reservation options we can present to our leadership and infra team. Goal: keep cost predictable while guaranteeing low tail latency during daytime clinical workloads.

Quick context:
- Expected steady-state inference: ~160 concurrent requests during daytime peaks (US EST 8a-6p).
- Typical prompt length: 512 tokens. Models: proprietary 13B family (we may finetune), plan to run 1–2 variants.
- Traffic pattern: weekday peaks with occasional scheduled burst testing windows (max 4x baseline for short windows).
- Internal compliance: must be VPC-connected and have audit log retention for 1 year.

What we need from you: three capacity options laid out as a simple "floorplan" table — minimum committed GPUs, burst/scale options, recommended ramp schedule and activation timeline, and any blocker items for on-prem vs. VPC deployment. Attachments or one-sheet preferred for exec review.

Target timeline: decision by end of November.

Thanks — Joel
Joel Pattinson
Head of Platform
Lumos Bio

---


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Joel — appreciate the concise context. Attached are three pragmatic reservation floorplans and a short SOW stub to help procurement: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx and Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf. High level summary below so you can paste into the packet.

Option A — Pilot / Low-commit (90-day)- Minimum commit: 8 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: up to 32 GPUs for scheduled test windows (auto-burst policy)- Ramp: immediate activation of 8 GPUs, burst cap available within 48–72 hours provisioning window- Use case: validate finetune + production smoke testing- Pricing: monthly reserved baseline + per-burst usage. Estimated monthly baseline cost range: $XXk–$YYk

Option B — Business (recommended for stable prod)- Minimum commit: 32 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: 64 GPUs available via short-term capacity lanes and scheduled bursts- Ramp: stage activation over 8 weeks (32 -> 48 -> 64 bursts enabled), full committed baseline online in 2 weeks- Use case: primary production serving with predictable latency and 2 model variants- Pricing: lower per-token unit cost vs. Option A; we include a 10% startup credit on first two months for a 12-month commit

Option C — Enterprise / Large-commit- Minimum commit: 96 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: 128 GPUs with multi-region fallback- Ramp: staged over 12–20 weeks with canary lanes and A/B traffic steering- Use case: multi-product rollout, strict SLOs and geo-bursting

Notes / restrictions:
- VPC deployment: includes private control plane, KMS integration; on-prem air-gap requires additional security assessment (see SOW).
- Audit logs: 12-month retention available as an add-on; standard retention is 90 days.
- Network: we recommend 10GbE minimum egress for the baseline 32-GPU option; see Ben’s note in the next message.

If you want, we can convert Option B into a one-page commitment slide for procurement and include estimated burn numbers. Which option should I flesh out first?

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx; Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf

---


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 14:43:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts — technical sizing notes

Joel / Aisha — adding a short technical annex to support the Options above. I attached network_requirements.docx with a brief checklist. Key sizing notes: 

1) GPU selection and effective throughput- For your 13B family we estimate ~13–16 tokens/sec per A100-80GB-equivalent GPU under float16 serving with KV cache. That maps to ~10–12 concurrent steady requests per GPU at 512 tokens average (depending on batching).

2) Minimum recommended baselines (conservative rule-of-thumb):- 8 GPUs: safe for pilots and small smokes (~80–100 qps sustained with aggressive batching)- 32 GPUs: production baseline for 160 concurrent daytime users at low latency assuming request coalescing and prefix caching- 96 GPUs: multi-product or multi-variant fleets with headroom for retraining jobs and throughput spikes

3) Burst and ramp behavior- We recommend configuring both automated burst lanes and a short-notice provisioning policy. Autofill burst usually requires a 24–72 hour warm-up window. For your scheduled test windows we can provision guaranteed burst lanes that come online in under 4 hours if pre-committed.

4) Network and storage- Baseline egress: 10Gbps for 32-GPU baseline. For 96 GPUs plan for 25–40Gbps depending on expected KV cache miss rates.

5) Safety and rollout- Canary lanes with sample-based quality monitoring; automatic fallbacks to smaller model variants if latency or error budgets breach.

If helpful I can run a quick capacity simulation with your request traces (anonymized) to produce expected percentiles for P50/P95/P99 latency against each option.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

Attachments: network_requirements.docx

---


From: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Aisha / Ben — thanks for the quick turnaround and clear options. After reviewing with infra and product leadership we want to move forward with Option B (32 GPU baseline with burst to 64) and the staged 8-week activation schedule. Two asks before procurement: 

1) Please send a one-page commitment slide + the SOW with pricing filled in so procurement can attach to the PO.
2) Please confirm DocuSign timeline — we need the signed agreement by Dec 3 to meet our kickoff date in mid-Jan.

We prefer the 12-month commit with the 10% startup credit you flagged. Also confirm the VPC deployment includes KMS integration and 12-month audit log add-on (we’ll cover the add-on cost).

If that all checks out I’ll get PO prep started and loop procurement for the DocuSign link.

> Joel — appreciate the concise context. Attached are three pragmatic reservation floorplans and a short SOW stub to help procurement: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx and Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf. High level summary below so you can paste into the packet.
>
> [snip — options A/B/C summary]

Regards,
Joel

title: Capacity decision & rollout checkpoint for CardioSense Dedicated Pool
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.inference>
To: Thomas Li <thomas.li@cardiosense.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-11-07T15:02:00-08:00
Subject: Follow-up: dedicated capacity recap & immediate asks

Thomas — thanks for taking the call earlier. Quick recap from our side so we’re aligned and can move to proposal: 

What you stated as the main pain points:
- Frequent latency spikes during peak inference windows (even with smaller batch sizes) impacting SLA to clinicians.
- Need strong tenant isolation for PHI workflows (multi-tenant model serving today but want dedicated pool).
- On-prem-ish networking requirement: VPC peering + BYOK for KMS; audit logs & retention for 12 months.

Goals you asked us to hit:
- P95 latency target: <= 120ms for 512-token requests in steady-state.
- Baseline throughput: ~300 requests/min steady with burst to 1k reqs/min during overnight backfills.
- Go-live target window: early Jan (you mentioned Jan 8–15).

Preliminary sizing notes (attached sizing worksheet):
- Recommended reserved baseline: 8x A100-equivalent GPUs (quantized 4-bit run for most models).
- Burst pool: 2 GPUs for short-term spikes (auto-burst to shared capacity if required).
- Canary plan: Gradual traffic ramp (10% → 50% → 100%) with automatic fallback to hosted model variant.

SLA & security: we can offer 99.9% uptime SLA for the dedicated pool + SOC2 evidence and audit logs. BYOK + VPC private control plane supported; we’ll include Ops acceptance checklist in the next doc.

Next steps we committed to on call:
1) I’ll share an initial commercial + technical sizing (attached).
2) Schedule a technical deep-dive (security + infra) for the week of Nov 14.
3) Draft mutual action plan for signatures and timeline (first cut attached).

Can you confirm: the go-live target window above still works and the procurement contact is procurement@cardiosense.ai? If anything above is off, call it out and I’ll update the sizing doc.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0193

-- attachments: call_recording_20261107.mp3; initial_sizing_estimate_cardiosense.xlsx; mudp_draft_cardiosense.pdf

Subject: Re: Follow-up: dedicated capacity recap & immediate asks

Ava — thanks, this aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from my side:
- Security: VPC + BYOK are hard requirements. We also need HSM-backed KMS and key rotation policies documented.
- Data: PHI retention rules mean we’ll need audit logs and a 12-month retention SLA; please call that out explicitly in your Ops checklist.
- Timeline: procurement prefers a Jan 10 go-live target; that gives us time for an internal security review.

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.inference>
To: Thomas Li <thomas.li@cardiosense.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-11-07T15:02:00-08:00
Subject: Follow-up: dedicated capacity recap & immediate asks

Thomas — thanks for taking the call earlier. Quick recap from our side so we’re aligned and can move to proposal: 

What you stated as the main pain points:
- Frequent latency spikes during peak inference windows (even with smaller batch sizes) impacting SLA to clinicians.
- Need strong tenant isolation for PHI workflows (multi-tenant model serving today but want dedicated pool).
- On-prem-ish networking requirement: VPC peering + BYOK for KMS; audit logs & retention for 12 months.

Goals you asked us to hit:
- P95 latency target: <= 120ms for 512-token requests in steady-state.
- Baseline throughput: ~300 requests/min steady with burst to 1k reqs/min during overnight backfills.
- Go-live target window: early Jan (you mentioned Jan 8–15).

Preliminary sizing notes (attached sizing worksheet):
- Recommended reserved baseline: 8x A100-equivalent GPUs (quantized 4-bit run for most models).
- Burst pool: 2 GPUs for short-term spikes (auto-burst to shared capacity if required).
- Canary plan: Gradual traffic ramp (10% → 50% → 100%) with automatic fallback to hosted model variant.

SLA & security: we can offer 99.9% uptime SLA for the dedicated pool + SOC2 evidence and audit logs. BYOK + VPC private control plane supported; we’ll include Ops acceptance checklist in the next doc.

Next steps we committed to on call:
1) I’ll share an initial commercial + technical sizing (attached).
2) Schedule a technical deep-dive (security + infra) for the week of Nov 14.
3) Draft mutual action plan for signatures and timeline (first cut attached).

Can you confirm: the go-live target window above still works and the procurement contact is procurement@cardiosense.ai? If anything above is off, call it out and I’ll update the sizing doc.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0193

title: Action required: VAT residency evidence + payment unlock matrix
preview:
From: Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: tax@solastix.com
Date: 2026-11-05T09:12:00Z
Subject: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Hi Aisha,

We have three outstanding Redwood invoices that are in our AP queue: INV-2026-0893, -0894 and -0895. Our local treasury needs confirmation of Solastix's tax treatment before we release payment — specifically: a copy of Redwood's EU VAT registration or self-billing agreement showing reverse-charge handling, and a brief note stating whether Redwood will be invoicing net of VAT under reverse charge.

I attached what we have on file (VAT_Registration_Scan.pdf) but our team says the scanned cert lacks the jurisdictional stamp. Can you confirm what additional evidence would satisfy EU/UK treasury so we can process the payment this week? Also let us know if you accept payment net-of-VAT in our region, or if a VAT-inclusive invoice will be reissued.

Thanks,
Claire
Accounts Payable
Solastix Analytics Ltd
attachments: VAT_Registration_Scan.pdf


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-05T12:04:00Z
Subject: Re: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Claire —

Thanks for the note and for sending the scan. We're keen to get this cleared for you. Short answer: we can enable reverse-charge (net-of-VAT) for Solastix once we have: 1) a clear jurisdictional VAT registration (with issuing authority stamp or a tax authority portal screenshot showing active status), and 2) a signed self-billing or billing authorization confirming Solastix will account for VAT locally.

What we'll do next: Ben (cc'd) will check our invoicing system for the issuing entity on the three invoices and confirm which legal entity issued them; Marissa will prepare a brief attestation from Redwood Finance confirming our treatment and whether we can reissue net invoices if needed.

If you can provide a tax authority portal screenshot (EU VAT VIES or UK gov gateway) for Solastix showing their VAT number active, that will dramatically speed things up. If you can't, the stamped VAT certificate or a signed letter from your tax/legal team is acceptable.

Proposed timeline:
- Today EOD (Redwood): confirm issuing entity + initial attestation (Ben/Marissa)
- Claire: upload authority screenshot or stamped cert by 2026-11-06 17:00 CET
- Once docs received: Redwood to reissue net-of-VAT invoices or provide self-billing agreement within 48h and request payment release.

If the treasury requires, we can also arrange a short call with our tax counsel — let us know.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Revenue Ops, Redwood Inference
phone: +1-415-555-0162


From: tax@solastix.com <tax@solastix.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
Date: 2026-11-06T08:27:00Z
Subject: Re: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Hi Aisha / Ben,

Attached are two items: Solastix_VAT_Certificate.jpg (official certificate) and a portal screenshot (VAT_VIES_Status.png) showing our VAT ID active per the local authority. Apologies — the certificate we initially sent was a low-res scan. The portal screenshot includes the issuing date and jurisdiction stamp.

Quick clarifications from our side:
- We are happy to accept self-billing if Redwood can provide the template and a signed agreement.
- For these particular invoices, our treasury prefers to receive net amounts (reverse-charge) so we don't duplicate VAT accounting.

Can Redwood confirm whether you will: (a) reissue net-of-VAT invoices, or (b) supply a signed self-billing agreement that allows us to process the current gross invoices as net?

Regards,
Solastix APAC Tax Team
attachments: Solastix_VAT_Certificate.jpg, VAT_VIES_Status.png


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: tax@solastix.com <tax@solastix.com>, Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-06T13:15:00Z
Subject: Re: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Thanks for the prompt upload — the portal screenshot looks good and should satisfy our auditors. Aisha and I reviewed the invoices: INV-2026-0893 and -0894 were issued by Redwood EU S.à r.l. (VAT ID: EU-99887766), INV-2026-0895 was issued by Redwood UK Ltd (VAT: GB123456789).

To get payment cleared this week, here are the options and our recommended path (payment unlock matrix):

Option A — Self-billing (preferred for speed):
- Redwood to send a one-page Self-Billing Authority (SBA) and sign it. Upon countersignature, Solastix can accept the current invoices as self-billed and release payment.
- Timeline: DocuSign out today, countersign by Solastix finance => payment release within 24-48h after countersign.

Option B — Reissue net-of-VAT invoices (reverse-charge):
- Redwood reissues INV-0893/0894/0895 as net-of-VAT from the issuing entity (EU S.à r.l. / Redwood UK Ltd) and sends a short attestation note referencing the portal screenshot.
- Timeline: 24–48h after confirmation; payment release after Solastix AP processes the credits + new invoices (may add 1–2 business days).

Required docs (either option):
- The portal screenshot you attached (sufficient).
- Signed SBA OR confirmation that you will account for VAT locally (email from your tax team is fine).

I've created a DocuSign envelope and attached the link here: https://app.docusign.com/solastix/self-bill/12345 — Aisha will send the envelope with the Redwood signatory.

Marissa will prepare the short attestation note for option B if you prefer reissued invoices; otherwise please countersign the SBA and we'll route for immediate payment release.

Ben Carter
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
attachments: (none)


From: Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: tax@solastix.com <tax@solastix.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T16:42:00Z
Subject: Re: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Ben / Aisha —

Thanks, that matrix is clear and appreciated. We'll proceed with Option A (self-billing) to expedite payment. I have forwarded the DocuSign envelope to our CFO for countersignature; I expect the signed SBA back by 2026-11-08 10:00 CET. Once countersigned, we will release the three invoice payments the same day or the following banking day depending on cut-off.

One quick ask: can Redwood include a one-line note in the SBA referencing the portal screenshot (file VAT_VIES_Status.png) so our treasury has a single doc that ties the self-billing authority to the active VAT status? Otherwise treasury tends to ask for cross-referenced evidence.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Claire
